review_text: Good awful. The only positive is that this trimmer can be used in the shower. I don’t have a bushy beard and even so this trimmer resulted in a very uneven cut and I was constantly having to rinse the gears hairs out to make sure it kept working. Spend more and get a better trimmer elsewhere.
It’s a very old model. I bought the 1st one in 2010 and it was awesome. I think the seller stored it since then that it didn’t work at all. I had to return it.

This was apparantly an export model. The in structions were in Japanese
This purchase was intended to replace a Panasonic ER2430. The unit that was shipped ER2403P has a different configeration  for the heighth adjustment.  My old unit had settings of 3, 4,5,6,, and so on.  This model has settings of 3, 6, 9, and 12.
Can I get the correct unit shipped?.  I will gladly return the 2403 P
Works very good.  Instructions are in Japanese! not acceptable.
Highest quality, reasonable price and powerful without a wire.
Described as it is
I received this set as a gift and I am really pleased with it.  I love having a beard but have always hated trying to keep it trimmed, neatly.  Problem solved!
It takes a very long time to fully charge this trimmer.
Much better than previous model
Excelente tengo años usandolo y hasta hora no me a fallado lo volveria a comprar

other shavers i bought had batteries didn't last so i figured buying a panasonic product was a safe choice as they are a major global battery manufacturer. Still going strong after several years. whenever i go on vacation i can leave the charger home not worrying the shaver will run out of battery. I use the charger to trim my lip for my mustache trim my beard to length and shave my neck. Only use a razor and cream once or twice a year for weddings and such. I previously owned Braun products. I'd buy this Panasonic again if the one i have die tomorrow.
Not the best shaver. It has always seemed to struggle, very low power. I like the sleekness and design but performance lacks. Definitely pulls the hair.
Tremendo producto este es mi segundo ..el primero se me callo y lo rompi.. y lo compraria de nuevo si se rompe otra vez..

Bought this back in 2014 and mine FINALLY died in 2025. I only manually oiled it for the first year, but after that, just kept using it, rinsing it, and so on. I was able to bring this thing on 1-2 month vacations and it kept a charge, using it a few times a week.

The only thing I wish Panasonic would have done by now is attach a USB-C port to their latest model as opposed to forcing every to keep their proprietary charing adapter handy, or at the very least, allow it's battery to be charged using an existing wireless standard and/or form factor. As someone who travels, I hate the idea of carrying around some bulky adapter the entire time simply to charge it once or twice.

Great design team, excellent product, and while I'm excited to try the latest 2025 version of this, I do hope to see this design actually improved on and finally made smaller + USB-C.
great product.
6 years later and its still working.  I paid $40 for it Oct 2019...  $100 now.... WTF?
Works well

I really like this razor.  I lost my first one.  Went through a couple new ones then seen this one and bought it.  Soon as I bought it I of course found my first one, but ended up keeping both cause they are made so well.  I think my first one is pushing 10 years old and still going strong.  I changed the head from the old one to the new one, and just ordered a new head is why I left the review years later!!!  But it’s a great overlap razor that can handle it all.  With the adjuster you can go straight to eyebrows etc.

I purchased one of these in 2015 and have used it often for the past ten years. It finally became irreparable, and I'm about to purchase another. Hopefully the quality is the same. I'll buy another one in ten years.
It’s cheaply made!

My husband has had this beard trimmer for FIVE years now and it’s still works as if it was brand new. I’ve bought other brands for my son and they never last more than 6 months, so this time I made sure to buy the newer version of this one for him! Sticking with Panasonic forever now!!!
Bought May 2. By mid-June it won’t charge. Amazon won’t accept a return or refund. It’s junk; don’t waste your money like I did.

Seller is selling used items

Razor had hair in it and was dirty

This is the second one as the first one didn't work at all!!
Keeps charge for 10 minutes. Horrible. Dont buy.
Works perfectly, shipped safely

This trimmer feels like it’s made backwards. The on/off button and the length dial are on the opposite side of the trimmer head so it is difficult to turn on as well as see the length scale without turning the trimmer over every time. The lower portion is narrowly shaped and makes holding the trimmer awkward. The head itself is narrow but trim’s clean. I purchased this model over the newer ER-GB42-K because it just has a charging cord instead of the charger/holder which would have taken up a slot in my bathroom outlet. It holds a charge fairly well but the charge light does not extinguish when the trimmer is charged so you don’t know exactly when it’s done.  It looks like the newer model solves some of these issues and costs less. Unknown reliability but it is a Panasonic so I expect it will despite its quirks.

Purchased and used almost daily since Aug 2014, which is over ten years. The power button finally gave out.

I Iike that it's wet/dry and has built-in adjustable guard, so that I don't have to keep track of attachments.

I'm replacin with a newer model, ER-GB42-K.
it works perfectly
Love the design and battery life! Blades are sharp and hopefully last a long time!
I’ve used this product since 2016.  On my second one.
Works great , definitely a good purchase
Like the way it trimmed my beard
6 yr old and it's still going strong
Great  trimmer for your beard, use  dry or  wet.  Long lasting  battery, and  easy  charging.  I  bought a 2nd  for  back up, since it  works  so well
My husband and I both like that this trimmer. It’s nice that you don’t need additional combs or blades.
Holds a long charge.
This is a replacement for an earlier model.of same trimmer. So love its,ease of use and features. Highly recommend
My old one lasted 5 years, I usually get about 2 before the battery dies

I really like this little trimmer. It’s plenty powerful enough and does a great job with my coarse hair. I like the adjustability of the head.  The cutting head is easily removable, which is nice and I would recommend cleaning it out every time you use it.  I usually oil it every other week.  So far though I think it’s the best beard trimmer, I personally have purchased.

I had one of these several years ago. When it finally died, I swapped it for another product.  Not a good choice for me.  After 18 months, I came back to this clipper.  Good product, great battery life, easily adjustable hair clipping length options.  Glad I could still get it.

I purchased this back when I started a new retail job.  Was on clearance!!  The battery has degraded a tiny bit over time, but it still works just as good as the first time.  I came to see what a new one would cost, and I must say IT IS WORTH IT
My old groomer broke and so I find this one after a lot of looking. It works really well, adjusts and cleans easily and the charging stand is nice to have. Highly  recommend.

My original purchase of this trimmer lasted 11 years of pretty regular use. It kept my beard arranged properly, even though I probably should have changed the blades 8 years ago. And also 5 years ago. But I oiled them before every use, let it dry thoroughly, and it's served me well. Today, it finally died - I broke the power button somehow. So here I am ordering it again because while I just read a pile of reviews on other makes and models, they all just seem so needlessly complex. I don't want a 15 in one trimmer with titanium handle or something that can take out an enraged elephant. I want a simple trimmer that I can use while exhausted and without my glasses. And also one that has a good battery life (11 years is pretty good) both between charges and battery longevity. This beast does all that.

Out of the hundreds of purchases I've made on Amazon, this might be the best. I have received seven years of good service from it. I'm probably going to have to replace it now, as the blades have dulled, the battery drains and it is making a grinding sound.

I broke the ON/OFF button, so now it doesn’t work. It was good for general beard and mustache trimming with the trim guards attached. It was just average for fine detail trimming (especially on the mustache) without the guards, mostly due to the width of the blade head.

I thought Panasonic would be a good brand. This powerless lackluster trimmer barely cuts. I thought it was not charged, but after an overnight charge same poor performance.  Wish I'd have spent $50 for something better.
Bought the trimmer for my husband for his birthday last year and it no longer works. Internal components have broken .

Per my husband: this is the best trimmer I have ever had. He keeps his beard short and this unit works perfectly for his needs. Since we have been searching for a long time to find the right trimmer, he is a happy guy now. Would recommend.
This thing is super cheap. Do not buy very low quality
It is a good product, but it doesn’t last for long periods.
Charger stopped working after 2 charges. Support website doesn’t work.
Excellent quality!
Quiet motor and very easy to use!
Very easy to use and clean!

What’s not to like? I bought my first one in November of 2014 and used it 1-2 times a week to trim my facial hair for 9 years so far. Does a decent job on the body hair as well and I used it to cut my hair on my head during the pandemic. I just started to notice some loss of battery life and hoped they were still making this beast. I had also dropped mine a couple of times and it wasn’t cared for as to the standards and it’s kept on working like a champ. Heck, I even took a picture of it so I could see if I could find the same model and wrote a review. I will definitely be buying another.
Just as described. Works great.

This duo is great.
The amount of power is fantastic. The grooming razor has excellent adjustability so if you just need to trim a bit u can dial it up and lean over the sink…
For the bigger shave, dial it down and  take it into the shower and shave your whole beard right off…

I’ve had the trimmer for a few months and overall I’m happy with it! I actually got mine from eBay at a much better price!

Perhaps in future Panasonic could make the trimmer with a digital battery gauge…that would be so much better I think.
Very difficult to use and not user friendly

My husband loves this razor so much that I bought him another one!

Thanks again Amazon!
I’ve been using this same trimmer for close to a decade now, just ordered my third one. Very clean cut, 10 adjustable settings, easy to clean out.

I've had my Panasonic Beard Trimmer  for over 3 years now and it still works very well. Many of the electric shavers I've bought lasted a year at the most, usually only 6 months. This trimmer looks great, charges quickly and cuts my beard just as well now as when I purchased it. The dial up adjustable height gives you whatever length you want, very easy to change the lengths for different parts of your beard. I highly recommend it!

Must not have been designed with a very nice long-life battery. Yes, all batteries will lose their life span eventually, so its to be expected. I did love this thing until it went caput quite suddenly. The ergonomics, waterproofing, and adjustable head without having to remove parts to lose is my favorite feature hands down.

This will not last forever, simply becasye they did not make it easy to find and replace the battery.

Story: So it was time to replace the battery and you have to take the entire thing apart... ok fine, but then you get to the battery and it's like this special batery that is sealed with two pins on either side to perfectly fit. Looking online I can't find this battery anywhere. They say I can just "Insert one AA (LR6) battery", but when I search for this I am unable to find with with the prongs like they have this one. I'm thinking if I just add a normal one that it will no longer be a wet/dry style since it will not have the prapritary covering they installed it with.

I love this trimmer. It has been going for 12 years. I kept it oiled and always returned it to its charging stand.

But… The soft blue rubber handle has finally started to decompose. It has turned into a sticky oily-ish mess.

Still pretty awesome for surviving 12 years.

 I just ordered its replacement.
How could they have beard trimmers that only work in europe (220v) in a west sacramento warehouse ? Worthless to me.  I guess thats what can happen with huge warehouse issues. oh well.

The owner's manual says this shaver cuts a little longer than the indicator shows. Well, it's not a little, it's a lot less than indicated. If you want a "1mm" close cut or near that, better choose another shaver.

I had to write a review after 9 years, using this in the shower, on the road, this thing has taken a lot of abuse. I just oil the blade every few times I use it. FINALLY after 9 years the blade broke. I ordered a replacement blade for $15. This thing is a workhorse. I was tempted to buy the newer model which is like a third of the price  but frankly this thing is a beast.
Didn’t use.. seemed to be very good and nice but it came with a UK plug. So I returned.

My last Panasonic was almost 10 yrs old and was $30. They are literally making worse products.

The adjustable blade, depends entirely on 2x thin plastic tabs, that WILL break, as one has to remove it to clean. You can’t use it while it’s plugged in and it has a 1hr charge, ridiculous for 2023 and it can turn on while in your travel bag. Panasonics been making electric razors since 1950! Going backwards.
Sometimes pulled facial hair which eased up as I slowed down as I shaved. Wonderful product

This unit does not come with a box full of pieces to get lost.  It's completely self-contained, and raises and lowers the guard with a dial on the case. It sits in a charging cradle with no wires to fuss with. The blades are replacable although I have yet to wear one out.  The battery life is plenty long enough to do my entire head.  Panasonic has always made quality products and this one is no different.  I own two and if one broke I'd go buy another one.

i was hoping by spending twice as much, it would be quality but this is CHEAP plastic.  I can't slide the guard off and i'm afraid it will snap.  the trimmer pinches which is a not a problem i had with the cheaper trimmers from the major brands. very disappointed by the ripoff.  the charger stopped working within a month.  absolute garbage

Not sure if quality is going down or what, but I’ve had two of these.  The 1st one lasted about 12 years before the battery quit charging for me.  The 2nd one lasted 5 years & wont turn off.  It’s a great trimmer, but quality seems to be diminishing.  Not worth the price in my opinion.

Since I started going with the "shaved head plus goatee & mustache" look about 20 years ago I've tried a bunch of different shavers, most of which were difficult to use, didn't perform all functions well, or simply weren't durable over time.
I got this Panasonic almost 15 years ago. I don't use it every day, but it has been very reliable. The power button finally gave up recently.
I was pleased to discover when I reordered it that it's exactly the same model, right down to the convenient cradle-charger. Good to see they didn't "improve" it by switching to cheaper parts.

Norelco changed their whole line up and no longer offer trimmer like this with the guard that slides up and down. They all have guards you have to add, even the 9000 that does slide up and down. This Panasonic is almost perfect. Fits great in the hand, the cutting angle is perfect, and the settings offer plenty of beard lengths unlike the similar priced Norelcos. Only complaint is the power button is a little finicky and sometimes doesn't stay on after pressing it. Other than that it's perfect.

Trimmer is very good but the UK version is unexpected.  This is the second trimmer of this model that I own.  The first is over a year old and very good as is this one for a second home.  However, the UK version is not what I expected to get.  I could do without the clunky 220V to 110V adaptor for the plug.

UPDATED:  Finally reached Panasonic and Warranty process is slow and outdated, manual forms, PDFs, attachments, etc.,etc., etc.,..HOURS.  Had to pay to send non-working unit in for service under warranty ($12) .  The warranty department responded quickly (all emails go to junk BTW) and confirmed unit not working and they opted not to repair but to replace.  However, they sent a much cheaper product (about half the cost of what I paid) with a cheaper wall mount charger vs. the stand, etc.!  As a result, I have contacted Panasonic yet again and had to resubmit all the paper work and details to...get this... fix their own mistake.  Waiting to hear back about either getting my money back or the correct replacement.  DO NOT BUY PANASONIC it is a nightmare to deal with.

UPDATE:  Purchased January 2023 and stopped charging September 2023 - 9 Months!  Contacted Panasonic twice and no answer, third time I hear back and I have to pay to ship the item back to them for evaluation of warranty coverage.  Please think twice about buying this product, I think the the UK version dose not work well in the USA with the adapter.

ORIGINAL REVIEW:  Product is for outside the USA.  The package flap of the box says UK which might be the United Kingdom.  If you are in USA and do not want to use the included adapter stay away.

Not sure why this is sold in USA markets, i.e, .com vs .uk without mentioning it is wired for the larger 3 prong outlets found in Europe for 220V vs. the USA at 110V.  Even the directions are for outside the USA regarding charging times.  This trimmer dose not have a light that changes color to tell you it is fully charged.  Very, very frustrating for those in the USA.

Will upload photos after review is approved.

A very good beard trim or scruff removal. Eyebrows, sideburn straightening or back of neck cleanup. Holds battery well.

First of all, there is a new model that's like 10 bucks less, so try to find that one. Second, like a lot of things I get on Amazon, this purchase annoyed me. It doesn't say anywhere in the description that this model is from the UK. I had this same trimmer and it broke so I ordered this one to replace it. You have to use the adapter to plug it in here in the US. Big deal? Not really. But again, like a lot of things on Amazon, it was annoying and yet another item that's not as described.

My old trimmer just would not hold a charge. Took a chance on this and so far so good. Like the stand, like the feel and adjustments. Works well on my head and beard. Only one minor issue, this was a european model, but it does work on 110 and no for no problem.

Best beard trimmer ever! I bought it 8 years ago and it has been the most reliable hair grooming product I have ever bought!  it has finally started to show some wear. Battery life is still great. I think the machine and blades just finally stopped cutting smooth.
This is the second Panasonic Cordless Beard trimmer for me. Purchased the previous one about seven years ago and it got me through about 2,000 shaves. Very reliable.
For beard and body this trimmer hits the mark.  Been using it for 5 years!

I had done a review for this item a long time back but apparently it was lost in the ether somewhere along the way.  Back then I was, as I still continue to be, fairly happy with this item.  It works for what I needed and even years later it continues to work as intended.  It isn't perfect, but for me it did and does work enough to help trim up the fuzz that I let grow, at times, out of control on my face.  That it has lasted over a decade is a testament to it being worth a favorable rating.  4 stars from this reviewer.

On my second one, the first one finally doesn't hold a charge very long after many years. Great for touching up the mustache and detail work around a hair cut. Very easy to hold and is not to heavy to make it clumsy. Never needed any service or sharpening - always did a great job of trimming.
I got this back in 2014 and it’s still going strong I use it for the lady bits

This is the second time I ordered this trimmer. The first one was back in 2016, so lasted 6 years. Only reason was the rechargeable battery gave up the ghost. I took it apart and it's basically a AA with attached prongs on both ends. I could have easily looked up that specific battery and replaced it but after 6 years I damaged it and rather started fresh.

Anyways, this thing is reliable, battery does last long (about 4-8 trims on one charge), and apparently could take a beating and keep on ticking. Hopefully the second one last just as long. My only worry is that they didn't decrease the quality to "save on cost" over the years. If they did, I wouldn't have mind to spend a bit more for the same exact product.
I love the product!
love this thing, high quality and lots of adjustments, holds a long charge.
Both of mine broke after a years use. I will be trying a different brand. This trimmer is junk.
I appreciate the ergonomic considerations…particularly for back of neck trimming.
This is the second one I bought first one lasted over seven years.  Great trimmer although the price went up over 35 dollars since the last one ai bought

I ordered this beard trimmer based on the glowing reviews. And it does do a good job of trimming and edging. However, I didn't expect the hassle involved in its maintenance. You are instructed to oil the blade head (in 3 places yet) before AND after each use. To do that you have to carefully remove the blade head, oil it, and carefully replace the blade head. Not too difficult, but a pain in the ass! With my previous trimmer, I just had to blow off the hair, and brush out occasionally, and it worked fine for almost 7 years. So, not thrilled with this trimmer.

Judging by the overwhelming number of positive reviews here, I feel certain that my trimmer may have had a manufacturing defect or some other anomaly. Anyway, this trimmer would literally slice open my skin when used without the guard to shape my beard. It would cut regardless of what angle of approach I used or how gentle I was. I've had a beard for 25 years and have gone through uncountable trimmers, and NEVER has my skin been cut in this manner.

Nothing to dislike, as far as I'm concerned.  Cuts well with very little pulling.  Easy to clean and use.  I've only used dry, however,  so not sure how well it works in shower.  Don't see why there would be any problem though.  I rate this a good buy!
You have to lubricate the blades before and after each use. Does not feel good when you shave pulls on hair

First and foremost, I’m sorry, Panasonic Beard Trimmer…

I did you wrong, you see we were together for almost three years. We showered together every night and I always got you wet. But did I take care of you and treat you like you were important?

No, after all those years I started to notice you getting a little hairy and a little smelly so I discarded you, like a trifle.

I then got a new beard trimmer and while she said she loved to go in the shower I could barely get her wet before she was completely useless. I began to grow shaggy and unkempt. People started noticing.

In a desperation I thought of how well you had treated me for all those years. I looked and there you were, just as I had left you. I put my hand into you drawers and laughed as it had been a year and there was no way I could still turn you on.

Under my fingers I found your button and you surprisingly sprang to life.  Not knowing how long we had together I dragged you to the shower, getting you wet and to my delight you lasted till I was finished and completely shorn.

You pretty much have me as a customer for life and hope everyone else has similar happy endings.

TJohn
It's weak! Takes twice as long to shave as my last one due to numerous passes over the same area!
It was a complete waste of money. If you have a thick beard.....forget it. It won't and can't do the job.

This is a good looking, quiet, long lasting razor. It cuts various lengths well. I'd have given it 5 stars, but it is a bit difficult to cut a clean, straight line between the mustache and the upper lip as well as at the bottom of the sideburns. It takes some skill to get these cuts right.
Trimmer did not cut it pulled my hair. RPM’s were too low. The pointy blade tips cut my skin. It did not work well at all.

This is the one to get when it comes to grooming and not break the bank!
Precise cutting from a trim to mowing through a thick beard. Also, it can rinsed out with water all clean and ready for the next use.

I’m sure the clippers would have worked wonderfully…
But unfortunately i had received a USED product that still had someone’s hair speckled throughout the blades.
I would not recommend buying this product from amazon.
I use it daily to trim my beard and cut my hair very short. This is my 2nd. The previous one i had it for three years but battery started to drain pretty quick, although i used it heavily.
Very weak in power and hair gets tangled

Size of the shave head is perfect for beard trimming, and without the cover can also be used for more precision around tight spots.  It is also small and light and easy to use and clean.
It doesn't trim evenly.  It shreds my beard with "hooks" that catch on everything like a microfiber wash cloth or towel, fleece lined jackets etc. that painfully pulls on the hairs.
This trimmer has lots of settings and is very easy to use. the charger makes storage on the counter easy and out of the way. Attractive design both beautiful and well constructed.

Umm this was worth the 39.99 I paid in 2015 but they have jacked the price to 75.99 for a beard trimmer. Don't waste your money. There a thousand other products out there for half the cost that do the same exact thing this does. I really do like this and glad mine still works. By reading the reviews it looks like price went up and quality went down. Mine still works just fine except the bladed have dulled.  I came here to replace mo=ine but am looking somewhere else.

This was a nice razor to use for the 3 weeks I was able to use it but unfortunately it stopped working correctly shortly after purchase. My suggestion would be to buy a different razor as the replacement/repair process to get this one fixed or replaced is just beyond ridiculous and has left me without a working razor for weeks and still with no date of a new one in sight. I have sent email after email and made phone calls on top of that and I have finally been given instructions to now send it off ( at my cost ) to a repair center with no idea when it will return. this was a nice little razor but because of the high difficulty in getting it replaced if something was to go wrong.... The battery is just always dead no matter what. I’d rather have a cord and a beard trimmer that actually works than something cordless that is never charged.
It works exactly as advertised.
This razor does a great job for beard trimming, however after a year, the battery stops holding a charge. I’ve bought 3 in 3 years. Also the price went up 100% in 2 years!
Used it everyday for a year now and lit works great
This works amazing. My girlfriend even keeps the short hair around my Peaky Binders haircut short with it.
Poor quality, weak motor, trimmer head will not reattach after removing to clean. Waste of money.

ive always been the guy that buys the cheapest beard trimmers from walmart but after having to buy a new one every year i decided to do some research to find a solid trimmer i could have for a long time. after doing some looking this was rated one of the best everyday trimmers and nothing has ever been more wrong about a product. this thing is cheap and does a TERRIBLE job trimming my beard. literally every time i use these i have to go back and go over several places that the trimmers hit but just didnt cut. im always finding areas in my beard where the god awful blades missed serveral hairs. i keep my beard pretty short so its always obvious when the blades miss a spot. on top of the terrible blade quality the one guard it came with broke within the first week of using it. it still works but to have the one guard break isnt a good sign. ive been using this trimmer for the past year hating every time i use it and now it wont hold a charge longer about 10-30 seconds before dying. it takes me about an hour now to get a complete trim now because this things battery is so bad and the charger is incapable of charging the battery fully. today its taken me almost 2 hours to get my beard trimmed because the battery keeps dying and i keep it on the charger when im not using it.

dont waste your time or money. buy something that your barber would use on a client. ive bought way too many "cheap" (if you can call an $80 trimmer cheap) trimmers over the years and they're never worth it. buy a quality product.
This was of the best purchases in my whole life.

With that said, this product breaks down after 2 years. And it sometimes jab your skin hurts. It’s def not worth 100.

The sound is also a clue, as it runs down.  It feels good in your hand, and is attractive, easy to use.  Cleaning is easy as it can be rinsed under running water.  The blades are too far apart and it misses alot of hairs, taking multiple passes to get a somewhat even cut.  I would not recommend this product.

The guard is garbage, on 4mm I had received a skin close save in several spots due to the guard being so so widely spaced between teeth. The shaver as a whole feels great, the guard is a big fat what qere you thinking.
Purchased July 2014.  Worked as advertised.  Precision cutting of beard and mustache, quick charging.  Will buy again if something happens to the original item.

I’m comparing the Remington MB6850 and the Panasonic ER-GB40 because they’re both ~$50 trimmers I tried. Buy the Panasonic, or something else.

STUBBLE TRIMMING EFFECTIVENESS. The Pana 19 levels of adjustment and I can get it to the perfect height for my stubble. The Philips is sort of slow, but it will produce beautiful stubble after about 5 minutes. The Remington offers 11 different levels, but it doesn’t trim them consistently. It leaves my face with a patchwork even after 10 minutes of trim time. Because it takes so much work, it leaves my face irritated.

FEATURES: The Remington wins here because of its vacuum, which works really well! It avoids the beard trim spray of which the Panasonic is certainly guilty.  I found that the vacuum feature works well, but be careful not to tip it. The Remington includes a second trimmer head. The Philips doesn’t include any of that, but it does have a nicer charger cradle.

BATTERY: About the same. I can get about 4 full trims on battery for each, but it seems to be better in the Remington. You’ll need it for that one too since it takes so long to trim.

FIT & FINISH: The Remington does not feel like a premium product. It’s clunky and full of hard plastics that looks terrible. But the vacuum and canister is nice to empty. To be honest, the Pana didn’t immediately strike me as a premium product, either but compared to them Remington you appreciate the lack of fake stainless steel and hard-touch plastics everywhere. The Pana rests nicely in the palm, too, and I’ve come to like the understated nature of it.

This trimmer is actually perhaps not nearly as bad as reviews suggest, it's basically a series of choosing the right 1 trick pony application and bending around it's awkward/not very user friendly limitations/backwards operation well enough to not break it in the process...in order to reap the uh...benefits?

First off, the positives:

1.) The battery life is ridiculous, as in it's the longest lasting on a single charge beard trimmer I've ever owned. Although it's competition is notoriously weak, making this competition sort of sad. I still want this battery for my mobile phone. I've been through three Norelco's that after the first 1.5 years were pretty much pointless to not use without the charger frequently, then just leave it on the charger, wired all the time basically.

2.) It's sharp enough out of the box, but absolutely requires lubrication to stay that way. This will come in the form of a drop or two of the included oil (let's face it, most guys lose this pretty quick/can't be bothered to do it with much regularity me included) and a splash through good old fashioned H2O, water in the common tongue, and suddenly it works well, actually really well considering the next plus.

3.) Again as it's intended usage as a "wet trimmer" it's extremely waterproof, can dunk the whole thing and be fine. In essence this should be thought of as strictly a WET trimmer. Drop the DRY usage from your mind entirely, Panasonic commands us to do this by design. Splash it running under the faucet before you start, rinse a few times while trimming. This causes 89% or more of the trimmings to stick to the guard, also lubricates the blades enough for a surprisingly smooth trim as wet hair trims easier than dry...hence all the soap and water when using a traditional razor, which I do as well for the rest of my face (Personally don't keep a full thick grizzly Adams beard either). Then just rinse it off with most of the trimmings going down the drain, pull the guard off (when in the full down position!) and give it a quick hand scrub/rinse the last time. Once you get used to this sort of mind shifting routine....oddly...this trimmer starts to work...really quite well.

Now, the negatives...which seem mostly design flaws to be "different" over function, not to mention introducing highly annoying learning curves...for a trimmer.

1.) By placing the height dial measurements on the back, somehow reacclimating the adjustments to Australian-toilet-crikey-blimey-flush-goes-the othaway-mate, everything anyone has learned about trimmers goes out the window. Up is down, down is up, the clock runs backwards, etc. Raising and lowering turns are opposite from intuition. You gotta look at the back now to see where you are at...and it takes an annoyingly long time to adjust to this if you have been using anything else. I frequently go between moustache and chin heights to no guide straight trimmer for side burns and mustachio over lip, so I adjust a lot. Making this frustration escalate to the point of wanting to smash it, then set is on fire. (Should have been listed as a positive, other than this critical frequently used piece, it's actually built like a tank) Even after finally getting all this right consistently, I still have occasional words with it, especially after a night of drinking. But's I'll say: it's very accurate and adjusts to the millimeter.

2.) Can't be over stressed. The trimmer guide requires you to Lower It All the Way before removing it OR YOU WILL BREAK it. Again, nobody is quite prepared for this, will think it's stuck at midway or highest and try to force it, thereby destroying it immediately or over short time wearing it down to break. Didn't think I had to read the freakin manual (RTFM) on a beard trimmer so much these days...but I do, otherwise you have to get your head around it prior (by reading this review perhaps) if you want it to last more than a a couple days. Then it might last 15 years.

3.) The charging stand indicator light is Red all week. Red when you first put it in. Red when fully charged. Red. Always and forever as long as the trimmer is in it. A Battle Star Galactica Cylon staring bright and deadly at you in the bathroom, ever vigilant, ever watchful. Amazing cheap efficient LED technology in strips for my computer for 14 bucks with a rainbow of colors that blink and breath at my command...this trimmer could not afford one LED with battery charge integration? Fortunately (and clearly by way of quirky cost saving design?), it won't be in the charger often, if you respect battery life/memory, I've gotten 20-30 trims off it without recharge. Even then it just goes a bit weaker but actually still works. Which out of common courtesy is when I finally place it in the charger overnight. Then it's good for another 25. Seriously, this thing almost rivals expensive Nicad power tool battery tech if treated as such. Can toss it in the bag for trips with confidence pretty easily without needing the charger even.

Now that I've somewhat irreversibly altered my routine to suit this trimmer's design whims, is it fantastic? Not really. Is it actually a very good to great AWAYS WET trimmer when adjust carefully and operated per a design that rudely altered the way I trim and cleanup arguably for the better...well yeah...sorta did. Will I get another superior sensibly designed, better all purpose trimmer when I find it and leave this one in the shower (or in a cabinet for long periods of time) for random shower man-scaping needs? Yes.

This is not the trimmer for someone who travels a lot.  The charging stand is tippy and awkward with an unnecessarily long, heavy cord.  The charging light just tells you it's plugged in, not whether the trimmer is actually charging or the trimmer is fully charged.  There is no charge level indicator on the trimmer itself.  The electrodes that interface the trimmer and charging stand are set high up on the sides of the trimmer, just where you'd expect to be holding it.  They stick out sideways like the electrodes on Frankenstein's neck.  Hence the "feel" of the trimmer is not "ergonomic", in spite of the ergonomic look.

On the positive side, the trimmer is very quiet, just a low hum, so you can use it late at night or early in the morning without waking people sleeping nearby.  However, you have to oil the blades after each use, so if you travel, you need to carry trimmer oil in your luggage.  There is no travel case to protect the trimmer itself, or your luggage from accidentally spilled oil.

I bought this without carefully looking at the pictures or considering what the charging stand looked like, or whether I wanted a "charging stand" at all.  Bad mistake.  This charging stand isn't suitable for the countertop near your sink because the lengthy, heavy cord takes up so much space on the counter.  If you store it the typical narrow drawer on either side of the sink, it takes up too much space in the drawer.  Having a "charging stand" means that you can't plug the cord into the trimmer and use it corded when the power runs low.  Lack of a charging level indicator means you won't know the charge is low until the power level suddenly drops, then it takes 20 hours (!) to recharge it.

It's just poorly designed, all for looks, not functionality.  I returned it with no regrets.

I had a pretty unique college career, before I settled on my final career of Engineering I studied both marketing and industrial design. In Marketing I learned that a good looking product can be sold and that even something that doesn't work well can be made to be perceived to work well just like the emperors new clothes.

I can only think that the reviews of this product that give if 5 stars are suffering from this mild form of hysteria.

Why? In industrial design I learned that form can follow function or function can follow form. I come from the former school, when I spend a given amount of time trimming my beard using a trimmer in different directions across every area of hair on my face I expect the hair on my face then to be all the same length when I'm done.

Clearly the person who designed this trimmer had absolutely no concern for how it works, as I am sitting here in work after paying the better part of $50 for a trimmer that has left clumps of hair of varying lengths throughout my beard even after I spent an excessive amount of time both last night and this morning attempting to use the trimmer.

My skin is actually sore from pushing down to try to get my beard hair to contact the blade through the guard.

The trimmer looks good, but its guard pushes hair down and away from the blade flattening it and keeping it from being cut. Compare this to a standard hair clippers used by a barber, the guard has no flat parts and acts like a comb pushing through the hair against the way it lies and exposing the hair evenly to the blade.

My wife threw my hair clippers away, she thought this trimmer was so "pretty" that she even threw the box and the receipt away and refuses to return it. You can rest assured however its coming with me the next time I go to the outside garbage.

I am for the moment stuck looking like a vagrant at my job because Panasonic didn't bother to see if their trimmer actually trimmed beards.

So if you want to look cool on Instagram when "trimming" your beard and you want something that looks impressive on the shelf definitely buy this thing.

If you want something that actually cuts hair I would strongly recommend a standard hair clipper like what you find at the barber shop, because it actually works. Cutting my beard with a hair trimmer takes less than ten minutes and its cut evenly and neatly.

I've spent over an hour last night and this morning trying to make this garbage thing meet its stated purpose and I have pieces of hair hanging into my mouth from my mustache, and I have long scraggly patches all over my beard.

Shame on you Panasonic for releasing this product, I will never buy anything from you again.

I bought this trimmer over a year ago  and am in the market for a new one.  Overall it is a very functional beard trimmer, but there are 2 main things that have me looking for a new(er) unit.  I keep my facial hair trimmed (beard & mustache, clean neck) and use this unit about once a week to do that.  It trims well and is easy to clean.  I've never used it in the shower though it is designed for that as well.  Here are the 2 things that I have issue with on my unit:

1.  When the trimmer is set to the max (10) the guard is VERY difficult to remove, and pretty much impossible to reinstall.  It has 2 tabs that must fit in to slots on either side and at that max setting, it's a real bear to do.  Sure, I just roll it down to the 5 or 6 setting, and it's easy.  But if I forget and start to remove it, one side comes out easy while the other gets jammed, and now you just have to tug it and hope you don't break it.

2.  Mine takes a VERY long time to fully charge.  I would estimate it at 30 hours or more.  I don't have to recharge often;  maybe once every 6 weeks I would guess.  So battery life is excellent.  But that one time I needed to trim up before going out with my wife, and I had not trimmed in more than a week... and the battery died.  I thought "no biggie, it should get enough juice in 1/2 hour.."  NOPE.  So now I drop it back on the charger every month to "top it off" and make sure that doesn't happen again.  I do not keep it on the charger since I don't want to "overcook" the battery and shorten the life of it, but maybe I should.

Overall, I'm satisfied with the unit.  it does the job I need it to, and so long as I watch out for those two issues above, I've had no real problems.  So why am I looking to replace it?  Because those 2 "gotchas" really annoy me.  My last trimmer didn't have as long of a battery life, but if I was "caught out", 20 min on the charger gave more than enough to get cleaned up.  it also had changeable guards that popped on & off like a cover.  It comes down to preferences, and having tried this unit and other, I prefer the removable guards.

One last thing... the charging cradle is odd to me.  With the way the trimmer gets inserted it just seems... backwards... a bit awkward... to me.  I travel every 6 weeks on average and prefer a simple charging cord to carry, not this extra bulky cradle thing.  Maybe the designers thought "oooohhhh, we'll make it look all fancy so it's on DISPLAY and guests can OOOHHHH  and AAHHHHHH over the beard trimmer..."  Sorry, I'm not that fancy.  Function wins out for me 90% of the time.  Panasonic has done a very good job, and they will never get everyone to like every aspect of their product.  I don't hate it, but after 18 months of ownership I'm looking for those features that matter more to me.

While this has a black head to it, it is made of a shiny material that reflects light and I need to constantly move around to not catch the reflected overhead light.

This trimmer kit includes everything you need to trim hair from all those odd places such a nose, ears, eyebrows, facial hair, and pretty much any other place where you need a trim. I have used a variety of different powered trimmers over the years but this one has the widest selection of trimming attachments.

The set comes in a nice presentation pack that is useful for storage.  The motor is certainly powerful enough to drive the heads through the thickest of hair without that irritating, and sometimes painful, snagging you get when the blades stop mid-cut.  No such problems with this one.

The usual rounded nose hair and straight ear hair attachments are included but I like the fact that there is also a 'beard trimmer' attachment which is useful for trimming the hair above and below the lips when I haven't shaved for a few days.  The eyebrow trimmers a great as well because they set a safe depth so you can't scalp them!

I did experience one issue when the nose trimmer attachment became stuck on the head and wouldn't detach. I let everything dry out and was then able to remove it although the blades detached from the outer shell.  I was able to reassemble it fairly easily though and have not had a repeat of the problem.

The only other improvement I can think of would be the inclusion of a small bottle of cutter oil.  I've found that a regular application of oil keeps the blades sharp and spinning and have my own bottle but many others likely won't.  This is particularly important if the trimmer is used in a wet environment.

Overall, this is a good trimmer set and I can recommend it.

This little set intrigued me so I decided to try it out.  I shave my head but grow my beard and mustache out, but keep both fairly short.  While I've used the nose hair trimmer and it works fine, I'm actually using the angled trimmer the most.

I don't like having my mustache hair growing over my lip, but it's a tad tricky finding a trimmer that can fit in the narrow space between mustache and upper lip without threatening to trim off another part of my mustache or press on my lip.  This attachment works beautifully for this exact scenario.

It's got enough power to cleanly snip the hairs without tugging, but it's not so aggressive I worry about it grabbing and cutting my upper lip.  And it's small enough to carefully maneuver around without worry of accidentally trimming into part of my mustache that I don't want trimmed.

My only gripe about this set is the fact that it uses a AA size battery.  So many portable trimmers/shavers use rechargeable lithium batteries these days, and lithium batteries have far superior performance compared to alkaline batteries.  Sure, you can get yourself a lithium AA size battery, but those aren't cheap.

If the battery isn't a big deal to you and you need a small, precision trimmer, then this is a good one to consider.

I have lots of micro-USB cords laying around for various devices, and it is a bother to have to stop and recharge something in the middle of using it. I have plenty of rechargeable AA batteries around too, and it will take just a few seconds to pop one of those in as needed.

I will probably not want to travel with this, but the small size will make it easy to store. The price, $28.61 as I'm writing this, seems reasonable for a nice little grooming tool.

URBANER MB-980 Waterproof Facial Hair Grooming Kit for Men, Cordless, Battery Operated Ear and Nose Hair Trimmer, Compact Eyebrow Trimmer Beard Shaver is for tiny, detailed hair trimming. This set is very tiny, the blades are very small. The trimmer body is very smooth, so it easily slips in your hand while using it, especially if it’s wet. There needs to be some grooves on the sides of the trimmer itself, so it won’t slip. There are three heads- a cone shaped nose hair trimmer, a side straight blade eyebrow, ear hair trimmer and a straight edge for any other tiny trimming anywhere.

There are also 2 combs for the side edge blade and a comb for the straight edge blade, for longer cutting.  Also included is a tiny brush for cleaning and the batteries in the trimmer are included. The set itself feels flimsy. The blades are really too tiny to cut much hair. I suppose this would be good for someone who has very fine, very little hair to trim, but it is pretty useless for a person with normal hair. The tiny 3/4 inch blades barely stick out for actual cutting. The average person may find this too tiny for trimming, so I have to give it 3 STARS for a person with fine hair.

Do NOT NOT NOT buy any men grooming products, especially the kits, from this Hong Kong based company - Ufree. Looks nice, comes with an attached blade (has own fitted plastic combs available) held in place by 2 screws - not easily detachable. Once screws removed, the plastic & spring assembly underneath blade plate springs apart - you are never going to get it back together. Why remove? There are numerous other attachments that allegedly just snap into place where the original attached blade assembly is. Nope. Not going to happen. These other attachments also have their own sealed assemblies with screws and fitted plastic combs that DO pop-on / pop-off. The Ufree site, User's Pamphlet, and YouTube videos do not closely show how this is all supposed to be done - just show the fitted plastic combs popping on and off. Ufree customer service sends email saying it's an Amazon issue and auto chat says someone will review the issue someday. The numerous emails now received say Amazon will fix it. It is NOT an Amazon issue.

The Ufree Beard Trimmer is a good personal trimmer that does what it says at an affordable price.  Although there are a few minor quirks that are worth mentioning, I would recommend this trimmer to a friend.

The device feels nice in the hand; solid, with a good heft to it.  Attachments snap tightly to the base, leaving virtually no chance for clippings to get into the works.  The power/speed button is easy to use (much better than a slippery slider switch), but its location may cause accidental operation while changing trimmer heads.

As a light user who trims a goatee two or three times a week and usually shaves once weekly, this has been a good personal groomer.  I've tried all of the 6 trimmer heads that are included, and each performed well when used for its designed purpose.  Like any multi-use tool, it's important to choose the correct trimmer head for the task at hand.  For example, the shaver attachment is not a lawn mower.  Read the instructions.

Changing a trimmer head requires a fair amount of force to both remove and attach the head, but once it's latched into place it is solid with zero wobble.  I've accidentally turned the thing on almost every time I've changed a trimmer head.  I suspect this is a known design issue because the instructions attempt to describe a specific way to hold the device when changing the head, which doesn't really work for me.  It feels more natural to hold the unit with the (easily triggered) power button facing my palm, and that's the problem.  It would be helpful if the button were slightly recessed to prevent the accidental triggering.

The included storage/display stand is not particularly useful.  There is a spot for each of the various heads and attachments, but they are tightly packed and the pieces do not lock into place.  This makes selecting the thing you want a bit clumsy, as I invariably knock something out of place, causing it to fall onto the counter.  Annoyingly, there is no dedicated slot for the included cleaning brush, which I use after every trimming session.

Although the device comes with a USB-C cord for charging, no charger is included.  Any standard cell phone charger seems to work fine, and I'm sure most people have one or two spares lying around, so keep this in mind if you want to charge the unit in the same location where you use it and your wall outlet doesn't include a USB charging port.

Overall, the usefulness and modest cost of the Ufree Beard Trimmer outweigh these minor complaints.

Here's an honest review:

I was excited to try the Beard Club PT-45 beard and hair trimmer, but unfortunately, it didn't quite meet my expectations.

Pros:
- The trimmer has a sleek, sturdy design and a user-friendly battery interface that displays the charge percentage.
It comes with a variety of colored hair combs, which is a nice touch.

Cons:
- The blade quality is lacking. It’s not sharp enough to provide a smooth, clean cut, which is disappointing for the price point.
- The attachment combs are difficult to slide on and off, requiring a lot of effort, which can be frustrating.
- The trimmer is heavier than expected, and its RPM isn't sufficient for a crisp cut.
- Even a budget, no-name brand trimmer I tried outperformed this one in terms of cutting quality.
- The blade can pop off easily, and reattaching it is a bit of a hassle, especially since the lowest setting isn't skin-tight.

In summary, while the trimmer looks good and has some nice features, the performance doesn't justify the cost. I wouldn't recommend it if you're looking for a precise, professional-grade trim.

The reason for this is because without using one of the plastic guides, you can go to 2mm length, the smallest plastic guide is 3mm, and the lowest setting on the unit itself is 0.8mm. This trimmer works well but it lacks enough guides and built in adjustability to make this truly great. The trimmer itself can adjust from .8mm to 2mm.  Most of the guides are 3mm apart but the two that are important to me are 4mm apart. There is a 6mm guide and the next size up is 10mm. The 6mm guide gives you 6.8mm to 8mm. The 10mm guide gives you 10.8mm to 12mm. So, there is a 2.8mm dead spot between 8mm and 10.8mm. That is a huge gap for a shorter beard. The other guides being 3mm apart give you a dead spot of 1.8mm which on a much longer beard is not much of an issue. But with a much shorter beard that 2.8mm gap is an issue.
Ideally, the guides for this trimmer should be 2mm apart which would then allow for full coverage. Unfortunately, more guides are not available. If you keep your beard in the 9mm range, this trimmer is not for you.

However, the trimmer doesn't click in place and doesn't stand perfectly upright in the dock. It leans pretty heavily and makes me concerned about whether or not it will tip over.

The trimmer does have redundancy for the power supply, though. Even if something happened to the dock, the power cord can plug directly into the trimmer. Which is a nice feature.

All in all, if this thing cost $20 it would be a good purchase. But for $70+? I'd say look elsewhere.

The MANSCAPED Beard Hedger stopped being able to turn on after less than 2 years. During this time, I used the trimer twice a month with satisfactory results. I am giving a 2-star review because I believe 2 years is too short a lifespan for this product.

I bought this tool after reading a 2025 review of beard trimmers by Men’s Health. I have been using for a little over a month and would recommend against buying, for two reasons that were not addressed in the review that lead me to make this purchase.

1) The battery does not seem like it is going to last long. Often the motor wavers like the battery is about to die. It hasn’t died, but it also lives on the charger and I would expect the trimmer to act fresh and energetic when used for 5 minutes. Instead, I usually wonder if it has enough juice to last through the trim.

2) One of the features is using a single guard for all of the lengths. It works ok, but there are two clear drawbacks. One is that the comb is too far from the blade to stand up all the hairs even at the fairly short length of 2.5 (out of 10). The result is random long stragglers along the jawline, which are annoying to find later. I do lots of passes from multiple directions and they still surprise me. The second problem is the guard cannot trim under the nose, so I end up with an area the I cannot get with this trimmer, and have to get another trimmer or use small scissors.

I feel like this trimmer doesn’t really save me much time over my previous one and I would return it if I could. It is nice to not have multiple guards but the solution doesn’t address trimming needs for a short beard. Perhaps they will improve the guard and battery.

I recommend skipping this one. Hope this helps someone.

The Beard Hedger is a well designed razor that brings convenience to my daily routine. After a few weeks of use, I can confidently say it gets the job done, but there are a couple of small problems that keep it from being perfect.

Pros:

The adjustable wheel is a great feature. It’s very easy to use and it eliminates the hassle of constantly swapping out different guards. This makes it great for shading your beard.

It’s cordless and waterproof, which makes it super convenient for wet use. I’ve used it in the shower a couple of times without issues. The build quality also feels very sturdy.

Cons:

While the motor works fine for regular maintenance, it struggles a bit with thicker facial hair. You may need to go over areas a few times for a truly even trim. I notice that if I don't shave regularly, the razor struggles cutting three to four weeks worth of hair in one go.

The battery life is decent but not outstanding. I expected a bit more longevity between charges for a trimmer at this price point.

Overall, I’m happy with the purchase. It performs well for most trimming needs. Just be aware that it is not a powerhouse for dense beards. Still, I’d recommend it to someone looking for a sleek and easy-to-use grooming tool.

I got a Braun cordless and it does a good job shaving, but can be tricky if I have been a week or more between shaves. That was the reason I purchased this beard trimmer. Leary of electric hair clippers near my throat and lips, I decided I would set the guard a distance from my skin and "sneak up" on my facial hair. The hair cut easily, no pulling or tugging and my throat and lips remained intact. Static caused the clippings to cover the trimmer, a problem I have seen on many electric hair clippers. Turns out these are able to be used in the shower so, no problem with cleaning up. I should mention that they are not noisy, and they have a USB "C" charge port, very nice. I'm very happy with my purchase.

Let me tell you about my journey with The Beard Hedger, the Swiss Army knife of facial hair management. This little beauty has transformed my beard care routine from "mountain man meets bird's nest" to "distinguished gentleman with a hint of rugged charm."

First off, the ergonomics are fantastic. It fits in your hand like a well-trained hamster - comfortable, precise, and unlikely to escape during critical grooming moments. The blades are sharp enough to make my previous trimmer weep with inadequacy, and the motor purrs like a content cat that's found the perfect sunbeam.

The waterproof feature is a game-changer. Now I can finally achieve my lifelong dream of trimming my beard while pretending to be Jacques Cousteau in the shower. "Ze magnificent facial hair must be maintained even in ze depths of ze bathroom!"

Clean-up is a breeze, which is ironic because using my old trimmer created enough loose hair to form several small tumbleweeds that would roll across my bathroom floor like I was staging a Western in my powder room.

However - and here's where we lose that fifth star - the maximum trim length is only 10mm. Yes, my fault for not reading the specs more carefully, but come on! Some of us want to maintain a beard that says "I could survive in the wilderness" without actually having to go there. For those aiming to channel their inner Gandalf, you might need to look elsewhere.

Despite this limitation, The Beard Hedger has earned its place in my grooming arsenal. It's reliable, efficient, and hasn't once tried to eat my face, which is more than I can say for some other trimmers I've owned.

Bottom line: If you're not trying to recreate ZZ Top's aesthetic, this is your tool. Just remember, 10mm is your ceiling - anything more and you'll need to either get creative with your trimming strategy or accept that you've outgrown the Hedger's hedging capabilities.
