Took multiple passes to trim my beard in one spot. Longest setting (10mm) takes me about a week to grow. Charged it for 2 days before using it, and still started to die about halfway through and couldn't handle my beard.

But hey, it looks good I guess. I'll be returning it.
With attachment, this trimmer does not cut as close as I prefer.
The shave is nice however given the size of the cap you dont really reach the bit below the nose.

I don't love it. I was using a trimmer my ex GF had bought for her dog and never used. That thing could cut! This is weak compared to that. I do like the adjustable length dial. Its accurate and easy to use. But I don't like how if you remove that part to try to use just the clipper part to say, clean up your neck line. It doesnt work.
I can't return it, because I used it. But i doubt Ill buy any other manscaped products.

I expected a lot more out of this trimmer for the price. I really should have went with a different option. Does not adjust for any real length. I can grow in one month what the longest setting on this trimmer cuts at. Highly disappointed.
A few gripes, it's a little large for a beard trimmer it's more like a barber tool used for lining. The shears are a little pokey and could be a little more narrow. It does cut well.

I've been using this trimmer in tandem with a Norelco 7000 series since June 2023.  My Manscaped trimmer no longer holds a sufficient charge to use more than 3-5 minutes and struggles to cut due to dull blades.  I'd say this trimmer works at around 10% based off of being 100% at time of purchase where my Norelco is still around 70% (in my opinion based on my own personal use and attempt to use them equally over time).

Now I am not saying it's not a good trimmer or leaving a bad review. I am making a comparison based on value for your money against my personal best choice in a similar range.
Overpriced for an average product.
It's a good trimmer but the battery stopped taking a charge after 8 months of twice-per-week use. Refuses to charge.

I initially had a great experience with the Beard Hedge trimmer, which I purchased in March. The trimmer was effective and easy to use, and I was quite satisfied with its performance during the first few months.

However, after less than 5 months of minimal use, the trimmer unexpectedly stopped working. I used it fewer than ten times and only needed to charge it twice. Now, it no longer turns on or shows any signs of charging when plugged in.
The trimmer was never dropped, exposed to harmful chemicals, or subjected to any form of damage. In fact, despite being advertised as waterproof, I rarely exposed it to water.

Given the short lifespan of this product, I'm disappointed and cannot recommend it. I would have expected a much longer period of reliable use, especially for a product at this price point.

This was too bulky to use.  I wasn’t able to get a close and consistent cut. It’s light weight and does not feel like a high quality item in your hand, which I expected based on the price. I went back to my Philips.

I don't know what everyone's raving about, I find this gives me a really uneven shave when maintaining a 1/2" beard, and it has a REALLY hard time cutting my curlier, stiffer white hairs. I missed the return window so I'm stuck with it, but right now this is a pass for me.

The product has performed as expected for the price.  I am satisfied with the face trim results…although the 0.5 setting does produce some slight variation, which is likely present on other length settings but is most noticeable on the shortest (0.5).

The case looks and feels nice.  However the zipper broke the first week.  I would greatly appreciate a replacement case!

I wouldn’t say that I’m a hairy man, but the body hair that I do have is CURLY. This manscaper does a fine job of straightening my hair before cutting, leaving a nice, even length. It’s only draw back is it’s rather clunky size.

Be careful, .5 looks like some peoples 5 o’clock shadow. However, it also let it cut my lip while trimming.

Side by side to my old trimmer, realized my old one is closer to a 1.5 with just the guard.  At 1.5 it did not cut my lip anymore.

It is great having so many increments to choose the desired length. Unfortunately the guard does keep it from getting right at your nose. A very quick snip with small scissors will get that spot.

That said, it took 2-3 passes to trim max. Very fast and wider area at a pass it seemed. So overall happy. Much faster than several other brands and styles of beard trimmers I’ve had in the past.

My old trimmer was an old cheaper brand that just broke but had for many years replaced it with the manscape and thought it would be better by the hype I seen on it but not that good hair gets caught in guard and stops cutting evenly when that happens unless I'm using it wrong not very impressed
I am concerned on using the beard hedger, the ability to adjust a consistent height of the beard.
Doesn’t trim short enough
Like everything about so far

My old beard and mustache trimmer died. I have had a beard and mustache since High School never once shaving it off. This new trimmer will take getting used to for old timmers for the angle of the trimming blade has become much steeper over the years. If you are not careful you could come very close to shaving your beard off. The weight and feel is great, only the angle of attack leaves much to be desired. You can tell it is designed for the fashion of 2020s with close cut beards. However I stopped being a boy long ago. I would give this 4.0 stars if I could, it's a solid product, just requires mindfulness.
The beard trimmer works great but the handle on the carting case broke on the first time opening the case.

The razor itself is very well made and so is the case but the zipper pole that’s attached to the zipper broke on the second time I zipped it. I’m very disappointed with that for the amount of money I paid. Handsome, giving only a four star rating
After several months of great trimming, it  suddenly just died. Wouldn’t charge and will not turn on. Disappointing at best would not recommend.
Love this trimmer, but that battery went bad after 1 year of use, I use it to trim my beard and shave my head.
If I could grow a super thick beard but otherwise just get the hedge trimmer.
Works as described with good ergonomic function
Good trimmer, but needs more length options
Loved it until it suddenly just died. Had it about 4 months and it just stopped. Wouldn’t charge and will not turn on. Disappointing.

Beard trimmer itself cuts very well and has a nice feel in my hand. But the truth is the blade end of the trimmer is just too big and bulky in order to reach the area immediately under my nose and trim my mustache properly. It works just fine and has an acceptable level of noise and the battery. Life is good. However, not being able to trim the hairs under my nose on my mustache was a deal-breaker. I ultimately returned the item.

It looks well built. However if you’re looking to trim your mustache  or trim sideburns etc. it does not cut to the skin.Also be careful how you tilt the trimmer attached to the unit it cut a divot in my mustache. No directions on exactly how to use were enclosed.
Great case and easy to use.

I have not opened up the packaging yet....but I'm disappointed. I purchased this item that was almost double the price of comparable units due to the fact that I loved the Made In USA verbiage on the sales page. When it arrived the packaging stated Designed in California ...crafted and manufactured in China.
So.... the Made in USA on the sales page was a big old pile of BS!!!!.
Not sure what im gonna due with this Chinese thing.
Trying g to trim my beard it's not very sharp

This trimmer is slim, powerful, has good battery life, and comes in a nice travel case.

It only works for longer beards though. If you set the guard for anything less than about 4mm it trims at a 1mm length. The size and design of the guard do not allow you to fully trim under your nose either.

I like to keep a short beard (about 2.5mm length) and this trimmer is terrible for that.
The product seems to work just fine but the cool travel case it came in the zipper literally broke the first time I opened it… makes me question the rest of the quality

My first time using it I got knocked a couple times using it without the guard but then again I was in a rush. When it comes to trimming you can press in the guard or you will get hairs at different lengths. Overall it feels modern and it’s a nice size to travel with. Battery life is good too.
This is a nice trimmer but its way to large for any detailing. Also the highest level it goes to in 10mm which is kinda short. Other than that its good quality and comes with a nice case.

I went with this and spent more than many other options thinking it would be a good buy and last. It works well and easy to adjust, but it slipped out of my hand one time and now it sounds like a real lawnmower and the teeth bite me constantly.

Only issue I had was the case zipper the piece attached to it broke off , it still can open and close but all it took was the first attempt to open it and it broke so easily so I just now have a key o-ring attached to it  lol

I like that I can trim beard and also cut my hair on my head with dialing in a number. I use 0 for beard and a 7 for hair. Battery life is average, maybe a week or two. It’s not good for lower regions of body though and it cuts my chin area underneath.  If i want a really close shave though this is not it. I will first use the manscape and then go over with a regular razor.
woorks ok
Battery last for a long time before needing a recharge I would recommend this product to anyone. Yes, it was a little more expensive than the others but the others you will waste your time.
I like the trimmer it does a really good job the only one down side it the guard rattles at a higher Setting like at  5or6 when it’s not against ur face but other then that it’s worth the money

I've been using manscape machines for a few years now. I was looking forward to this machine, and after a few months of usage I can tell you that it's just not as good as it can be. It's got great battery life. It trims fast, I really don't spend more than 10 minutes trimming. But the big issue I have, is the inconsistency when choosing which level to use to cut. If I use 3/3.0, it cuts certain areas of my beard evenly. But if I get to other certain parts, it sometimes cuts well, other times it cuts less than it should or more than it should.

The machine can easily be one of the very best, I think it simply needs a few minor modifications and the next model will be great. But that's also the issue. Why are we constantly having to buy a new model. Why can't the current model be the absolute best?

It's disappointing and surprising to realize the pattern that the company is following after also noticing the drastic drop in battery life from my other manscape machine, the lawnmower.

Alas, I hope I'm just a one off. And I hope others have a better experience. But based on the two manscape products I have, I can't say that I'm looking forward to the next machine they release.

This unit feels really good to hold and maneuver, but the blade cover is not great.  It is either confusing or cumbersome,  but getting the blade setting AND the comb cover coordinated and properly adjusted is a trick.  I suppose one you get it where you are getting the results you want,  you're good.  Just a bit of a hassle getting there.

I just started growing a beard.  This product works well for me now.  However as my beard grows, it seems clear to me that this will not be the best product for me in the future.  The dial only goes up to lengths of 10 mm.
Loving the design of this beard trimmer so far. Eliminates the need to hold onto tons of accessories and clip sizes. Comes with a quality case and charges quickly and easily.

This trimmer does ok,, but I found it best to use prior to showering, as the comb portion leaves your beard feeling rough and uneven because of comb feature... After showering and the beard hair softened and laid down properly, it felt fine.
Solid clippers. Cut beard with ease and even. Only downfall is edges. It wish it had a more precise cut for  these lines.
Does an awesome just with the guard. But doesn’t cut as close to the skin as others when you take the guard off
Pretty good for beard over all.  They just need a smaller precision trimmer for mustaches.

Does great! Easy to hold and use. My husband wanted it to manscape his whole body with the advertising it says it wouldnt cut you but first use it got him a time or two on his stomach. But maybe just need to get use to using a new clipper
It worked well until the battery died a few months after purchase.

This is a quality beard trimmer, but the major issue I have with it is that the length options are very limited.  This isn’t so much a beard trimmer as it is a “so you’ve decided to shave your beard off” tool.

Even on the longest length you are going to end up with a very short beard.  Honest I don’t even use it as a beard trimmer as a result because it just shaved it too short.  So now it’s basically just a pubes trimmer, which it does well.

So overall a quality trimmer, but I’m pretty disappointed with my purchase because I can’t use it as a beard trimmer as even the longest setting results in a very short beard.
Worked well and was easy to clean. Unfortunately, it completely died two weeks after the return window closed. It will not turn on and will not take a charge. The seller issued a full refund.
The on/off switch is very difficult to turn on or off

Ordered this, along with the Lawn Mower 5.0 (returning this also) and a travel bag for a really good price. Being a Manscaped product, I have the Lawn Mower 3.0, I figured I would be getting an excellent beard trimmer. Although it comes in great packaging, as do all of the Manscaped trimmers, I am not a fan. The trimmer head is way too wide, making it difficult to detail and the trimmer guard is way too big. I like my beard short and did try it several times, adjusting the trimmer head, but it didn't work for me. It would probably work better for longer beards, and I would recommend this for guys with longer beards.
The clippers wouldn't trim evenly, was given a prompt refund
Not as close as a shave as I expected
It is a good trimmer, no doubt. But does not do well for finer trimming, such as around the mouth/mustache. Going back to Wahl.
Works great, not so great for shaving head
Long lasting battery charge but cumbersome to use in small areas and mustache trimming.

I couldn't get a closely shaved beard with this thing. The .5mm setting just doesn't do it. Manscaped says to just press against your skin harder but then you're left with nothing but stubble.

My previous beard trimmers were far easier to get a short, even trim across my entire face. I wanted to like this product but I think it's lacking.

Had a manscape trimmer before, so I chose this model. Cuts decently on an older beard/goatee. Cuts okay but not as close as previous beard trimmers I've owned. Very HD construction.  Could use a head that cuts shorter than model included with shaver.

If you have a Beard that is longer then 1/2 inch's this clipper is NOT for you!

This is for close cut beards only.

Would be great if the company offer a bigger Guard for longer beards.

The clipper is light weight, and the blade is sharp and easy to hold in the hand, so if you want to free hand it is posable. All the other products i have from this company are great but this just did not work for real beards...
It's a decent trimmer but I think I got one that does not charge well - I'll be replacing for a second try ..
This is great for trimming the length of your beard but will not give you a trim and clean lines
Yeah, it’s nice. Not a Rolls Royce by any means—but it’s worth the price and will do the job.
Product was as described and works very well! Very happy with it!

I recently purchased this item… I used it once and it did a marvelous job, however, the blade stopped functioning properly afterwards. I reached out to the company and I got a replacement free of charge. I will rate this product and customer service at a 4 star review, the only downside why I took away one star is the fact that there’s no replacement blades on the market as yet to compensate for the regular use of the hedger

I really like the trimmer and how it allows you to adjust length. I have rinsed it off in the sink, and have used it in the shower, with no problem at all. My only complaint, and this really isn’t a complaint but a minor inconvenience is that I wish it came with a stand like the lawnmower. The travel case that comes with it is nice, but I would rather have a stand to charge it in.

Was looking for a work horse. Beard, head and body. This was not it unless your hair is thick. Pulls on thinner hair.

The build quality is amazing. It’s not cheaply made, just wish is came with an option to change heads for other types of hair.

Best though is that you can just wash it off with water and not have to worry about oiling it up or it rusting.
Use it opposite the grain of your beard…one star off for medium grade overall quality.
My husband loves this for his beard. Although it is really only meant for keeping your beard very short.

If you have a full head of hair, this is an ideal product for you. If you’re bald and like to keep your hair very short around 1-2mm. This is not the right product for you.
If you want your hair to be 0mm this product offers that, but longer up to 2mm I found to be problematic.
For body grooming it’s exceptional.
Overall it’s well built, high end and easy to clean.

Bought this because I wanted something a bit larger than my current trimmer, and it was on sale for Prime Day. Definitely works better than my cheaper model but I’d be happier if it would for work body hair as well. Definitely didn’t.  Glad I bought it on sale…

I've tried three or four different beard trimmers. My biggest complaint was that they came with three or four  attachments for trimming your beard which I always found somewhat confusing and really frustrating to use and manipulate. The Manscaped trimmer comes with one attachment and you make the adjustments on the shaver itself which for me is a godsend.
One of the best trimmers I have purchased.  Really enjoy that it holds a charge for so long.  Highly recommended.
The blade for this trimmer is not very sharp. Instead of gliding smooth, it tends to snag the hair.
It cuts like a dream, bladerunner good

This 4-in-1 Beard & Mustache Trimmer is not so recommendable. It has stopped working after two weeks. The quality is not so great, and even the price point is not so good too. Beware and do not waste your money!
Grooming kit comes with 4 different heads to get the look you want with. Its nice that its rechargable so you can use whenever you need it.

My partner and I use this interchangeably. I mostly use only two attachment and they work great. The charge last long enough for both of us to use it. I do wish it had some sort of smaller case so I don't have to keep the whole box all the time granted I'm just nitpicking at this point

Really like this set.  It is high quality and the blades cut well.  The nose trimmer (hard to find at first - under the cap on main part) is the best I have ever used.  The rest of the cutters are great as well.  Only complaint is the largest trimmer is not very big for trimming chest hair (I have a lot!)  it cuts great and does not pull but it takes a few minutes to cover a large area (like I have)  Issue is if they made a bigger head, it would need a bigger base and then wouldn't be as portable as this set it.  Since this is my travel set, it is not an issue.

Overall this product works great. It has several attachments and guides for them, and does a good job of cutting with almost zero pulls. I have had a few times trying to change the attachment out that it didn't want to come off very easily, but that could be due to it being new and the parts still tight fit.

My teenage son likes this product. It has everything he needs for his grooming needs. It is compact and stores away nicely in the container included. The shaving power is fine for his needs and I like that it is a product he will actually use. Getting teens to take care of shaving (etc) can sometime be a challenge but this product has elliminated me reminding him to shave.
It's very sturdy and easy to clean. Cuts very well also.
My husband thought this was going to be cheap because it looks a bit cheap but he was pleasantly surprised at how great this ended up being.
Holds a charge for quite a while and cuts well!

This kit came with separate heads, allowing me to easily switch between trimming different areas of my face. Whether I needed to tidy up my nose hair, shape my mustache, trim my beard, or groom my eyebrows, this all-in-one device had me covered.

Using the trimmer was a breeze. The separate heads were easy to attach and detach, providing a hassle-free transition between different grooming tasks. The trimmer's design was ergonomic and comfortable to hold, allowing for precise control and maneuverability.

I was impressed with the performance of the trimmer. The blades were sharp and efficient, effortlessly trimming and shaping my facial hair with ease. It provided a clean and precise trim, allowing me to achieve the desired look with minimal effort.

The trimmer was also easy to clean. The detachable heads could be rinsed under running water, ensuring hygienic maintenance and prolonging the life of the device.

Overall, I had a positive experience with this trimmer. Its multifunctionality and separate heads for each grooming task made it a versatile and convenient tool. Whether I needed to trim my nose hair, shape my mustache, groom my beard, or tidy up my eyebrows, this kit had everything I needed.

Pretty good, comes with many attachments.  I do wish the charger plugged right into the clipper, rather than having a charging base, since we keep it in a travel bag.  My only other complaint is that the foil shaver doesn't shave very close, but this seems to be typical of most foil shavers.  Other than that it works perfectly, and is convenient to keep in the car.

I got this for my husband and he was pretty happy with it. It works well and comes with different attachments for nose trimming, eyebrows, mustache etc.  It also can charge with a usb so no need for batteries. Overall a nice trimming tool

Before I talk about the actual device, I want to mention that this charges with a USB C cable, which is the current standard.  I have another grooming device that has a proprietary cable which I already misplaced rendering the other product useless until I find the cable.  USB C is the standard and the Urbaner uses it.  You can also plug the cable into the base and set the trimmer on the base to charge.

The previous generation of trimmers used a replaceable battery.  This one with the rechargeable battery is so much nicer.

There is a nice selection of attachments that are easy to swap out and attach securely.

The brush is double-ended to ensure you can clean out the attachments.
Came well packed looks pretty professional everything is exactly as advertised by the seller

This set comes in a nice, giftable box. The trimmer looks chic and has a good weight to it. It is simple, yet comfortable to use. The charging base gives a luxurious look to the trimmer, but in practice it would probably be more convenient if the trimmer had a USB connector to be used with a standard charger. One of the best aspects of this set is that it has a good variety of heads. The mesh head is not a common one that you can find in all trimmer sets. The trimmer is relatively quiet and works well. On the down side, the mechanical parts are all plastic and upon close inspection look flimsy. When I removed the nose clipper the second time, it came out in two pieces. Perhaps it is separable to make it easier to clean, but I don’t have time to put the two pieces together every time I change the head. At $55, this set is way too expensive in its class.

I am split between 3 and 4 stars. This is not as good as some of the 4-star trimmers, but it is better than some of the 3-star trimmers I reviewed. At the end of the day, a 4-star product is something that is not perfect but I would buy. This is something that is not bad, but I would not buy, especially at this price. Hence 3 stars.

This hair grooming has everything you need to keep you face beautiful. From eyebrows to beard. Also it’s cordless, rechargeable and stylish. My husband loves it. It make a great gift for Father’s Day , birthdays or any way you would like to celebrate the man in your life.

The Urbaner Facial Hair Grooming Kit is a good, well-made product but would not be my first choice if considering buying another kit of the same type.

At the cost of $54.99, it’s a good trimmer but doesn’t have the features that I personally prefer in my trimmers.  That being said, here are the Pros/Cons on the features of this product:

Pros:
* Product is waterproof.
* Product is cordless.
* Takes 90 minutes to charge on a USB-C connection for a 90 minute usage time.
* Can plug straight into device or use a charging stand (included).
* Trimmer head is removable and washable.
* Includes various items needed for full facial trimming – nose hair clipper, mustache trimmer, beard trimmer and eyebrow trimmer.
* Comes with a Cleaning Brush.
* Simple to operate.
* Does job as intended with no nicks, cuts or irritation.

Cons:
* Has no carrying case. Products like this should always include one because attachments could be easily lost.
* Cannot tell when device is about to lose battery charge.
* Item doesn’t say what RPM the motor has but seems on the low side.

I found the trimmer to work well trimming my mustache, nose and facial hair.  The shaver comes with a charging base and USB cable to allow it to be connected to a USB-C charging adapter (not included).  It isn’t obvious how to change the head pieces, so make sure and read the instructions.  I would have liked this to come with an AC adapter that would be suitable to use near a bathroom sink so that the trimmer could stay charged and ready for use.  I don’t have a suitable AC adapter in my bathroom.  The charging base is nice and heavy.

This cordless rechargeable grooming kit comes with a decent variety of attachments.  Among these are a nose/ear hair trimmer head, an adjustable (5 length) comb head, a blade foil shaver, a precision trimming head, and a couple two-position guide comb attachments for eyebrows, etc.  They also include a nice weighted charging base, a USB-C charging cord, and a cleaning brush.  The set comes very nicely boxed with everything in its place.  The box design would make this a nice giftset for sure.

After trying out the attachments I’m finding that the nose/ear hair trimmer works fairly well.  The foil blade shaver I found to be rather inefficient.  I kept going over small hairs again and again, and it just didn’t cut them.  The precision head worked well and I was able to trim my neck and get a well-defined edge.  The adjustable comb attachments were good.  The shortest setting seemed to give me the close but rugged short-beard look that I like.  The eyebrow attachments didn’t really do anything, but that’s probably because my eyebrows aren’t that long.

The weight of the device is good, and it is easy to hold and operate.  Some of the attachments come apart for cleaning, so that’s a plus.  The vibration and noise level of the shaver is a little annoying.  It’s much louder than I expected.

It’s a decent set and a nice grooming kit.  I think it would make a nice gift for someone, and overall, I think it’s decent quality.  Not all of the attachments worked well for me, but the ones that did seemed to perform well.

I really like this facial hair grooming kit.  It is small but powerful - the shaver removes even thicker grey facial hair.  It comes with everything you would possibly need for facial grooming - easily gets in and around smaller areas.  The charging stand is a plus.  The kit comes beautifully packaged - this would make a really nice gift.

I bought a ton of these kind of things over my lifetime and most of them are cheap garbage. I was really not expecting a whole lot from these. But I was pleasantly surprised. The main trimmer performs very well and while the motor isn’t exactly strong it’s not as weak as the ones that use a single AA battery. It’s performed relatively well and consistently. It doesn’t seem to get hung up as much as the battery powered ones. I love the fact that this one charges by USB. A few of the other attachments are useful as well. The precision eyebrow trimmer and sideburn clippers work surprisingly well. Not as well as my one blade but they will do A respectable job.

I haven’t really tried the foil head because I don’t really have a use for it I don’t expect it to replace a shaver of that type.

I said like this really needs to come with a custom fitted zip up case. Especially this price and it doesn’t so you’re liable to lose the accessories unless you keep them in the cardboard box which is really quite bulky. The charging stand is nice and has a good weight to it but the trimmer is so easily knocked off of the charger you really have to be careful. I think I would’ve preferred a straight plug and cable to the unit.

Still overall I was pleasantly surprised. It doesn’t have any of the gimmicks like a LED light or anything but it does a good job for what it’s supposed to do.

But the price, the price is a little steep for what you get. Especially because you don’t get a case. I’m sure there are trimmers just as good at half of the price. But then again there are also many trimmers available that are just simple wastes of money. So at least when you buy this when you know you’re getting something that will work. I do believe the lithium ion battery has a little more power than the battery operated one so I would at least look for a rechargeable one if you search for something cheaper.

This is a nice grooming kit with interchangeable heads. I like the angled clipper and the rotating nose hair attachments the most. The foil shaver works well, especially if your hair is not super thick, long, and or abundant. If you’re fairly low maintenance, the charge will last you at least one week (or more if you don’t use it daily). Then you do not have to bring the USB-C or charging dock on your travels, and you can leave behind any attachments that aren’t crucial to your trip. Just make sure to reserve enough time for the initial charge.

Although it looks fancy, I don’t have any feelings about the charging dock. It’s a nice touch but I would have loved to have a USB wall plug and storage case, especially for a set at this price point.

Pleasantly surprised that this beard trimming kit came giftable: not your typical display box that you would see in a retail store. Although, I wish that it had a little mesh bag to keep all components together.
The trimmer and components are of good quality and does its job well.
The handle is lightweight but I wish it would be thicker. The vibration was so intense that left my hands tingling.
The handle has a matt finish to help with your grip.
The included instructions do a good job in explaining what each attachment of 4 attachments is used for.
Overall, it is a nice set and I do like it.

This multi-functional groomer is cordless and perfectly lightweight.

It has several attachments for trimming all your body & facial hair.

You can use it in the shower or on the go.

Each charge lasts 90 minutes.

Perfectly small enough for travel use while being substantial enough for daily use at home.

Recommended.

I'm fairly ambivalent about this kit. It's cute enough, but ultimately it's underpowered for the features it attempts to provide. You have a low-powered motor with a variety of twist-on attachments. The motor is fine for nose hair removal. But the other attachments, which are supposed to remove body hair/trim are severely underpowered and inefficient.

The device is small enough that it's easy to maneuver in your hand, and that might be a plus if you're attempting to use this for very fine detail body grooming. It's reasonable for trimming back eyebrows, for example. But good luck removing any subtantial growth of hair with this: It's slow, and tends to get bogged down. Using it to clean up sideburns was not much fun, for example.

In the kit you get:

** Motorized device with interchangable heads
** Nose hair removal head
** Small clipper head with length guard
** Right angle clipper head with length guards
** Foil shaver head
** Nice weighted charging stand
** USB charging cable

I’ve been using the OLOV beard trimmer for a couple of weeks, mainly for beard maintenance and light body grooming. Overall, it delivers solid performance for the price point.

Performance & Power:
The motor feels strong enough to cut through thicker beard hair without pulling. It trims evenly and doesn’t snag, which is important for sensitive areas. The blades seem sharp and precise, especially for edging and shaping.

Attachments & Versatility:
The variety of guard sizes is a big plus. It makes it easy to switch between different lengths and styles. The included attachments feel lightweight but functional. For everyday grooming needs, the set is quite complete.

Battery & Charging:
Battery life has been good so far. A full charge lasts multiple grooming sessions. The digital display is helpful for monitoring battery percentage.

Things to consider:
 • The plastic guards feel slightly less durable compared to premium brands.
 • It’s not the quietest trimmer — noticeable motor sound during use.
 • For very coarse or dense hair, you may need to go over the same area twice.

Overall, this is a practical and versatile grooming kit that performs well for regular maintenance. While it may not replace high-end professional tools, it offers strong value and reliable results for home use.

**Overview:**
I recently received this beard trimmer and was initially impressed by its well-made appearance and the nice packaging.

**Experience:**
Upon unboxing the trimmer, I gave it a full charge overnight and eagerly awaited using it the next morning. Unfortunately, I encountered issues with changing the blade. The instructions mentioned pulling the blade off the razor, which seemed odd and difficult. After some struggle, I managed to remove the blade and attach a different one. However, the process was cumbersome.

The real trouble began when I attempted to remove the second blade. The top of the blade broke off in the process.

**Pros:**
- Well-made appearance
- Nice packaging

**Cons:**
- Difficult and awkward blade removal process
- Blade broke during removal

**Specific Example:**
The blade removal process as per the instructions is cumbersome and led to the blade breaking, which was quite disappointing.

**Conclusion:**
Due to the issues with the blade removal and the fact that it broke, I had to schedule a return. I would suggest reworking the product design to include a button for easier blade removal rather than having to pull it off. Unfortunately, I cannot recommend this product in its current state.

Been hesitant to pull the trigger on an item called “Ufree”, but that was “stoopit.”  Some comments made me “ascared” so I backed off for quite a while.  Ended up finally buying this trimmer on pre-Xmas sale along with the 2 year warranty-just in case.  Turns out, per usual, some commenters are just wrong.

Previously I’ve been using the Remington Body Groomer (hereafter RBG) for many years now, for everything.  It has a rare extendable body that’s great for reach, but the battery is suspect as it had to be replaced with a new unit early on 6-7 years ago.  I see it’s dying now, so I had to find its successor before actual death.  Definitely not going Remington again, so in steps this Ufree.  It is a worthy successor!  I’ve seen similar high quality cutters used by the stylists when I used to go for haircuts.

I’ve used this Ufree 7 weeks now, although only about twice a week to trim a goatee and shave the stubble down around it.  All I need it for.  It arrived charged @ 100% in mid-Dec, and I just dropped to 60% yesterday, so battery life is as advertised.  It is way sturdier, solid, and metal compared to the plastic RBG.  The heavier weight is a pleasure in hand and the cutting is way superior.  In fact, the comments that held me up mentioned pulling hair when used directly on skin.  Nope.  Wrong.  Just glides along with zero discomfort.  Don’t know what they were doing to generate that issue but I find absolutely NO PULL whatsoever using directly on face or body.  Just an easy, and very close shave on skin - much closer than the RBG - which is why I only use it 2X/week.  It shaves so good on skin, actually down to a 5 o’clock shadow.  Fabulous.  I seldom need a real shave anymore.  Plus, one of the trimmer blades is wider so my job is even easier/quicker now.  The stainless steel blades are really professional quality with precision and good solid build.

I’ve used trimmers for decades, and this one with the funny name is the most versatile & professional heavy duty solid product of them all.  It also looks great in bronze/gold and stands upright on the vanity.  Just grab n go n done.  With all the attachments and hair cutting ability it’s a one time purchase for everything.  Cannot recommend this trimmer highly enough!

The Ufree Beard Trimmer is a robust grooming tool that delivers on several fronts. Its heavy-duty build exudes a sense of luxury and durability, making it feel like a premium product in hand. The cordless design adds convenience, and the battery life is impressive, allowing for multiple grooming sessions on a single charge. The stainless steel blades are sharp and glide effortlessly through hair, facilitating clean cuts and precise trims.

However, it's worth noting that while the trimmer excels in trimming and shaping, it doesn't achieve an ultra-close shave. If you're aiming for a completely smooth finish, this might not be the ideal tool. Additionally, the trimmer is lightweight and easy to handle, but some users might prefer a bit more heft for added control.

In summary, the Ufree Beard Trimmer offers excellent value for its price point. It's a reliable choice for regular beard maintenance, provided you're aware of its limitations regarding ultra-close shaving.

There’s a lot to like here. First off, this isn’t just one clipper. It’s a full two-piece cordless setup that covers all your trimming needs from beard to bald fade, nose to neckline. The build quality is sturdy with a good kind of heavy that feels solid in your hand. They’re not light, but they’re also not awkward or fatiguing. The weight gives a nice sense of control while cutting. Both clippers have a strong grip texture that helps avoid drops, which is crucial.

Performance-wise, these clippers slice through hair cleanly without biting or pulling, even when your hands are sweaty. The stainless steel blades stay sharp and smooth. The USB-C charging is fast and convenient, and the LED screens are easy to read. I also appreciate the lock feature for travel. Everything feels thoughtfully designed.

The attachments are generous. You get plenty of guards for all hair lengths and styles, plus extras like a nose trimmer head, oil, a cleaning brush, and a detail trimmer. You can really dial in your look with this kit.

Now for the one real weak point. The included storage bag is pitiful. It’s a flimsy velvet drawstring pouch with no compartments and no structure. With so many attachments, this kit really deserves a zippered organizer or hard case. I ended up swapping it out for one I already had.

Pros
Two separate clippers included, not just one
Sharp blades that don’t snag or bite
Long battery life with quick USB-C charging
Comfortable grip and low vibration
Great selection of attachments including wide and precision trimmer heads
Clear LED screen and travel lock

Cons
Heavier than average, but still manageable
Storage bag is low quality and should be replaced

All in all, this set delivers great value. Just plan on getting a better bag to keep everything together. I’ll update this review if anything changes over time, but as of now, I’m really satisfied.

I was looking for a new grooming kit and this Ufree set has genuinely impressed me from the moment I unboxed it. The build quality is excellent, the attachments are comprehensive, and one feature in particular has allowed me to get the perfectly trimmed beard I've always wanted.

What I Liked:

Excellent Build Quality and Heft: The first thing I noticed was the solid feel of the trimmer. It has a nice heft to it that gives it a premium, professional feel, not like a cheap, lightweight plastic tool.

Comprehensive Attachments: I was very impressed with the number and quality of the attachments. This kit is so complete that I was able to get rid of my old standalone nose trimmer, saving me space in my drawer.

The Adjustable Beard Dial is a Game-Changer: The standout feature for me is the beard trimming guard with the adjustable dial. It's something I never knew I needed, but it provides incredible precision and makes it so easy to get a consistent length every time. This has resulted in the perfectly trimmed beard I've always tried (and failed) to achieve before.

Convenient USB Charging: I love that it charges via a standard USB cable. It means I don't have another bulky, proprietary charging cradle taking up permanent space on my bathroom counter. When it's done charging, I can just unplug the cable and put it away.

What I Didn't Like:

My only minor difficulty was figuring out how to swap the different attachment heads for the very first time. There was a slight learning curve to get the hang of it, but once I understood the mechanism, it became very easy.

Who Should Buy:

Any man looking for a high-quality, all-in-one grooming kit for their beard, nose, ears, and general hair trimming.

People who appreciate a tool with a solid, premium feel.

Anyone who wants the precision and consistency of an adjustable dial guard for their beard trimming.

Those who prefer the convenience and space-saving nature of USB charging over a dedicated cradle.

Who Should Skip:

If you only need a very basic, single-purpose trimmer, this kit might have more attachments than you need.

Star Rating: 5 out of 5 Stars

I am thoroughly impressed with this grooming kit. The build quality is fantastic, and the array of attachments makes it a true all-in-one solution. The adjustable beard guard, in particular, has been a game-changer for me. Add in the convenience of USB charging, and you have a product that delivers professional-feeling results and is easy to live with. Highly recommended.

I ordered this UFree cordless beard clipper and trimmer set to replace the old corded clipper that my husband has been using for many, many years. I have to admit that the fun embossed designs on the bodies really caught my attention.

This set comes with a dizzying array of accessories, most of which will likely not see any use here but are nice to have. The most appreciated ones are the variety of guide combs, which makes it easy to assure an even trim. The clipper blades are sharp and cut coarse beard hair with little drag, and do a great job.

The rechargeable battery holds a charge very well, with the power level barely budging even after a rather lengthy  grooming session. It provides plenty of power to keep the blades running at a steady speed, making for smoother cutting.  Both the clipper and the trimmer are of a shape and weight that is easy to handle, although the lack of a cord is greatly appreciated.

The narrower bladed trimmer comes in handy for areas where the wider clipper blade can be awkward, such as shaping in tight spaces, such as trimming around the lips and shaping side burns.

This set provides just about everything one needs for keeping a beard well-trimmed.

I love the packaging of these clippers.  It is black and silver and looks great and would be a great gift.

You get 2 hair clippers and a beard trimmer in this set as well as a ton of attachments, comb, brush cleaner etc.  These are rechargeable, so no cord while your trimming, because we all know that cord gets in the way.  Being cordless makes it easy to use.

These have a design on them, which is pretty cool.  They have some weight to them so they are not cheap.  They are not very loud.  All around it is a great set for a great price.

This Ufree Beard Trimmer for Men Professional with Hair Clippers, Barber Clippers and Trimmers Set is a nice set with a clipper and trimmer that are both cordless and rechargeable which makes it very portable and easy to use.  The clipper is slightly adjustable and both are very quiet when in use.  This set comes with a lot of accessories including a charging cable, comb, cleaning brush, clipper oil along with a storage bag.  This also comes with a set of clipper guards that are numbered to correspond with desired hair length.  Overall, this is a good set with lots of accessories that make this a great value that also works very well and is very functional.  The battery life seems to be last for multiple hair cuts and  relatively durable.

I got these clippers for when I cut my husbands hair and was super happy that it came with a second one for the face.  These were to replace our 20+ year old clippers that were handed down to us.  I really like these clippers.  I like that they are heavy, so they are nice to hold and you don’t have to worry about dropping them.  They are rechargeable, so you need to make sure they are charged before using them.  You will also need a charging block as it doesn’t come with one.  The battery life could be improved, but overall, it’s not horrible.  One other suggestion I have for these is a bigger storage case.  With all of the accessories that come with it, a hard cover, zippered storage case would be nice.  I have all of our is a tub with the other hair cutting accessories that we have, so that not a super big deal.

What I like about the razor shaver for face and hair: I get two devices for both face and head. The shavers can be used cordless or while charging via the USB to USC cord. There is now power supply; you will need to consider this if your gifting the device ( We have USB and USC chargers in our outlets so it’s not a problem for us to charge the devices). The cord is extra long making it easier to use the shaver while it’s charging. The oil with the cleaning brush helps with maintaining the device is in working order. There is an included comb to help with the hair shaving and grooming. Each shaver comes with a blade guard for the stainless steel razor tips. The hair shaver allows you to trim your sideburns, beard, mustache and there’s even a nose hair trim attachment. You have 8 additional shaver length comb guides for those who like more hair on their head ( we use the stainless steel for a close shave plus my husband shaves his head bald). The vibration didn’t hurt my wrist and the weight of the shaver did not cause hand/wrist fatigue. Both shavers buzz at a lower noise rate. The blades are nice and sharp giving a smooth shave. The battery has held up well for daily facial shaves and has yet to require a recharge as the battery can last for up to 150 minutes while the head/hair shaver can last 180 minutes. The power indicator and oil required reminders are appreciated and thoughtful reminders. There is a safety lock so the shavers don’t turn on accidentally while traveling and it has an indicator light to alert you when this mechanism is engaged. The LED screen is bright, clear and easy to read. You get a special beard stainless steel trimmer with an attachment from 1 to 10mm that you change by turning the dial. I have small hands and the razors felt bigger when I gripped them but were still comfortable when shaving my husbands head. The velvet drawstring bag can hold all the attachments and shavers for convenient storage and travel.

This hair clippers set is versatile as it allowed me to create all kinds of hair styles easily through my own creativity. The clippers have a quiet and powerful motor that glides effortless without pulling or tugging on the hair. The agronomical body provided a firm grip that is easy to hold while still allowing great maneuverability. The device has a decent size battery that lasted a long time before it needed to be recharged. The included guards are simple to replace and they ensured that I get the proper cut length. The steel blades are sharp and they provided a haircut that’s effortless and smooth without having to constantly stop to clean out the hair. This clipper also comes with attachments to trim my facial hairs and ears so this device is also capable of trimming other areas of the body. The fine-tooth comb blades ensured a closer shave that is also precise without causing any discomforts.

Good quality clipper set at a reasonable price. Picking them up for the first time they feel very sturdy and well made.  Plenty of attachments are included to get the desired length cut you are looking for.  Can easily cut your hair, clean up the hairline, trim your facial hair, remove any excess nose or ear hair that you may have all with this set. There was no pulling or tugging of hair while using this.  Just a nice clean cut.
Charging is easy with a USB-C port, and the battery life lasts quite a while.  So unless you are using this daily, you shouldn't have to charge these too often.
For the price and quality of the clippers, I would say that these are definitely worth the purchase price of just over $50 at the time of this review.

These clippers/trimmers are almost perfect. The only thing I wish is that they did not have that cheap looking embossed design of that bearded guy, it is just a step up from the dragon design. I can't use this in the salon, I would be ashamed to use it.

Just a generic knurled pattern or any other texture like durbar plate or carbon fiber would be much more professional looking.

The performance of the clippers is great, not zero-cut from factory but I was able to adjust it, not too loud and strong enough. The trimmer is also good with a great selection of guards, I just wish it came with metal clips so they would be more durable.

There's a lot of good qualities to this set. Most importantly, these trimmers are not easy to drop while maneuvering one-handed. Secondly, these trimmers doesn't bite at all, even when hot and a bit sweaty. This is now my weekly cleanup kit.

That said, while I like the battery life, I'm not a fan of how heavy these are and that, with so many attachments and parts, it doesn't come with a decent hard or zippered case. The bag it comes with is not adequate.

Here's what I noticed:
✅ Does not bite, grab or stab follicles. This is impressive, especially important for shaving more sensitive areas.
✅ Has excellent grip which is essential for a trimmer. (One drop will usually kill a trimmer blade in my experience.)
☑️ Heavier than I like but tolerable.
✅ Comes with every attachment you could want.
☑️ Doesn't come with a decent zippered bag or hard-case to store attachments or shavers.
✅ Smaller trimmer has a wide blade. This is essential and almost absent from the marketplace. Must-have. I don't want to be trimming all day.
✅ USB charging adds a lot of flexibility. I don't have to carry around a wall wart cabled charger.
✅ Power on/off button is physical and easy to toggle; Essential for serious haircutting or beard shaping because I'm constantly turning the unit on and off while working.
✅ Great value for the money.
☑️ Should come with decent storage at this price point.

This set it well worth the money, just get a zippered bag for holding everything together. If there are any changes in this set's performance, I'll report back in. 🤞 I hope you found my notes, pictures useful. Be well! ❤️🔥

This beard trimmer kit has three heads that cover beard, nose, and body hair trimming. The blades stay sharp and cut smoothly without pulling, even on thick hair. The motor is strong and can adjust speeds up to 8,000 RPM for fast trimming.

I like that it runs cordless for up to two hours after a 90-minute charge. The USB-C charging is convenient, and the LED battery indicator shows how much power is left. It is waterproof, so I clean it easily under the tap after use.

The travel pouch holds everything but feels a bit small. The power button needs a long press to turn on, which avoids accidental starts. The many attachments give many options but take time to change.

The Ufree 3-in-1 Beard Trimmer Kit includes the following attachments:
1. Precision Beard Trimmer Attachment: This attachment includes sharp blades and seven guide combs (1 mm, 3 mm, 5 mm, 7 mm, 9 mm, 12 mm, and 16 mm) for beard trimming.
2. Detailer Attachment: This attachment features tapered blades, six combs (3 mm, 6 mm, 9 mm, 12 mm, 15 mm, 19 mm), and left and right ear trimmer attachments for a close shave.
3. Nose Trimmer Attachment: This attachment features a 360° rotating blade designed for deep nose hair removal.
Other positive features of this shaver include:
• The battery is rechargeable.
• Includes a Type-C charging cable.
• A travel pouch.
• IPX6 waterproof rating. It can be used in the shower, with shaving foams or gels, and is easily rinsed under running water after use.
• It has an LED battery indicator that clearly shows the electric shaver's remaining charge.
• Includes a cleaning brush.
• It's easy to hold and operate.
Note: To maintain optimal performance, clean the blades with the included cleaning brush, let the electric trimmer air dry, and apply a drop of oil occasionally.
The only thing it's missing to earn a five-star rating is that it doesn't include a charging base or a storage case. Otherwise, the Ufree All-in-One Trimmer Kit is very complete and will save me time and money at the barber's. I highly recommend it.

I have a corded trimmer that was very heavy and difficult to use, so I decided to try this trimmer and overall you get a lot of value for the price.  It comes with a ton of attachments, you just need to push the trimmer head off if you want to change to a smaller attachment, so you can change to what you need pretty easily.  I both like and don't like that you have to push the power button for 3 seconds to turn it on.  It is great so it doesn't accidentally turn on if you have it in you bag traveling, but a little annoying to change one of the attachments and having to do it again.  I like that the batteries are rechargeable rather than having to supply new batteries whenever it runs out of charge.  Overall I think this is a good buy.

In general, I am not a huge fan of all-in-one grooming systems.  I prefer to invest in quality groomers with minimal attachments and purchase nose and ear trimmers separately.

This excellent trimming package may give me a change of mind.

I find this all-in-one grooming system quite impressive. Its powerful motor and sharp blades effortlessly trim facial hair. 15 different attachments allow you to keep your beard and moustache at the perfect length.  In addition, a nose and ear trimmer keeps unsightly growth at bay.

Initially, I was surprised that the trimmer didn’t easily power on. Instead, you need to press and hold the power button for 3 seconds. This is a great feature because it prevents the trimmer from accidentally turning on while carrying it in a toiletry bag or suitcase.

Once powered on, you can feel the trimmer's powerful motor.  In my initial efforts, the trimmer efficiently glided across the bearded area with minimal effort.  This is a trimmer that does its job well, and if you need an extra boost for tough trims, it can operate in Turbo Mode.

One feature that gives this trimmer a premium feel is its front digital display, which informs you of remaining battery power, Turbo Mode operation, and lock status.

The trimmer is rechargeable via a USB-C charging cord, which is included.  You need to provide a power brick.

There are a few things that prevent this trimmer from getting a 5-star recommendation:

First and foremost, there are simply too many attachments available, which necessitates a significant amount of trial and error when attempting to achieve the desired length for your beard.  I would have preferred a single adjustable trimmer that slides to length.

Considering this, these attachments can be quite cumbersome to remove and install. For instance, the nose and ear trimmer took me several minutes of pulling and tugging to remove.  Nobody has that kind of time on their hands to struggle with installing and detaching.

Although I still prefer a groomer with an adjustable trimmer over one with 15 attachments, I still consider this an excellent purchase, especially for a young novice purchasing their first grooming kit.  For more experienced users, this trimmer provides a very powerful and efficient grooming experience.

Sure wish I had gotten one of these sooner. Everything you need for grooming is in this package. So throw all your other trimmers, shavers away. And it holds as charge for a couple weeks of you need it to.

Let me start this review on a positive note, but unfortunately after that its going to turn mostly negative.

✅  From the outset, this kit impresses and sets a high level of expectation....it comes in various trays, one of which turns into a storage/charging tray that you can use to hold all the accessories.
✅ It's a beautifully designed piece - the main body is lovely to hold, ergonomic and waterproof. The pieces snap on magnetically and stay in place. The different widgets snap on and off the different heads effortlessly. It's really well designed - if Apple designed a shaver you'd expect this level of thought and UX in it.
✅ It's a very complete kit - many different heads, accessories, widgets and tools. Even has some lubricating oil to maintain the blades. This is a complete kit for and well thought out.

Now for the bad news - it's not very good at cutting hair.
In fact, some of the blades are completely terrible to the point of being useless.
And just so you can understand my perspective - male, decent beard all round (10mm sides to 25mm on chin), plenty of body hair. I normally use a 5 year old "brand name" trimmer with a few similar attachments that was slightly more expensive than this.
To measure this. I started each time with a clean sink then tried each blade, checked how much came off into the sink, then re-tried with my older trimmer. Ditto in a bath for the body hair.

Here is what I found.
👎🏼 The nose trimmer is 100% useless. Did not cut any nostril hairs. At all. With nostril hairs you can really feel the buss and there was no cutting action, and nothing in the sink afterwards. I then tried my old nose trimmer....lots of buzzing and cutting with clean nostrils afterwards.

👎🏼 The U shaped trimmer - tried it on 12mm setting first, did a patchy job of cutting my beard and made me look homeless. Then switched to 9mm to clean up the damage, similarly left lots of patches. At this point I was starting to wonder if there were some safety covers on the blades that I had missed, Im not sure how they can be this ineffective. But I couldn't find any safety features or settings.
👎🏼 Switched to the T shaped head on 9mm.....trimmed off more hair, illustrating that the U shaped blade had missed large chunks. Definitely an improvement, if I had to use this trimmer I would stick with the T shaped head
👎🏼 Then switched to my 5 year old trimmer on its 9mm setting - lots more hair came off and evenly, with my beard finally looking normal.

So ...100% fail for nose trimming and I wouldn't shave my beard with this under any cirsumstances.

I also tried the foil head on the softer hairs on the back of my neck and it failed to cut any of them, at all. It was slightly better on the coarser short hairs on my throat but also left most of them there. 100% fail.

I then switched to the body trimmer - here where things started to work well.
I found the medium guard good for main body hair, except the edges are a bit sharp and leave red marks on your skin.

I then switched to the close shaving guard for the private regions, this was pretty good also and I would use it.

Overall, I cant recommend this kit at all based on the performance of the blades, I found most of them to be sub-par and in some cases totally ineffective.

The various selection of blades plus and the addition of a hair catching cloak makes this an excellent purchase. The only downside is it doesn’t hold a charge for more than two uses, but it is rechargeable.
Everything works well, the only thing is the power button waiting 3 seconds.Every time you turn it on and off

There is a certain honesty in tools that do not pretend to be more than they are. The TRIMX Beard Trimmer, model TX-HL-KT521-DG, belongs to this rare category. It does not promise transcendence, yet it offers something far more useful: clarity. In the small, daily ritual of grooming, one confronts the mirror and, inevitably, oneself. This trimmer meets that moment with calm efficiency. It is balanced, precise, and reliable, cutting cleanly without tug or protest, as if it understands the futility of resistance.

The motor hums steadily, indifferent to haste, reminding the user that care requires patience. The adjustable settings allow for deliberate choice—stubble, trim, or beard fully asserted—each length a quiet declaration of intent. The battery life endures longer than expected, refusing to abandon you mid-task, which in itself feels like a minor rebellion against inconvenience.

In a world often defined by excess and noise, the TRIMX trimmer succeeds through restraint. It performs its duty faithfully, and in doing so, affirms that even the simplest acts, when done well, carry meaning.

I’ve used this grooming kit a few times now, and it works very smoothly. The motor feels strong without creating too much vibration, which makes trimming more comfortable. The attachments with silicone edges are a big plus—they connect easily and feel safe on the skin. The LED display is handy for checking motor speed and battery percentage, and even though I didn’t test the full 180‑minute claim, one full charge was more than enough for complete body grooming. The build quality feels solid in hand, and the Type‑C charging is convenient for travel since it avoids carrying extra cables.

The magnetic docking base is also useful because it keeps everything organised and charges the trimmer while it sits in the dock. It’s a practical setup for home or even commercial use. The only thing to be careful about is using the U‑shape and T‑shape blades directly on the skin—they’re very sharp, so attachments are definitely recommended for safer trimming. With attachments, the experience is smooth and irritation‑free.

Overall, it’s a well‑designed grooming kit with strong performance, good battery life, and a premium feel. A few small improvements could make it even better, but it still delivers great value for full‑body grooming.

Pros:

• Smooth trimming with low vibration
• Strong motor and solid build quality
• Useful silicone‑edged attachments
• LED display for speed and battery
• Type‑C charging and magnetic dock
• Good for full‑body grooming

Cons:

• Direct blades are very sharp and need caution
• Could improve blade edge design for safer skin contact

There are several really thoughtful, well designed additions to this pack that make this a really interesting option for anyone looking for a new beard trimmer. I need to give my beard a minor trim every two or three days, so I'm probably the target market for this product, and I was excited to try it out.

First of all, the rubberised tips on the trimmer guides are REALLY comfortable. This is a massive improvement over the years plastic guides that are usually supplied. The kit also comes with a charging tray, which helps to keep everything organised and your trimmer ready to go at all times.

I was also really impressed by the magnetised shaver heads that come with this kit. This makes it really easy to switch heads out very quickly. I wish more manufacturers would do this as it's very convenient. Likewise the lock on the on/off button - because you have to press it down for 3 seconds to unlock it's unlikely to go off in your bag while travelling.

My biggest quibble with this shaver is that it may not be suitable for all beard types. I don't have a particularly thick beard but I still had to give it many multiple passes before I got rid of most of the hairs I kept missing. This might be down to the power rating - it suggests that it cuts at 6000 rpm (6600 on 'turbo' mode) but to be honest it feels a little gutless. It was passable for a medium beard like mine but a few of my friends with really thick beards would have no chance with this. The blade is held on with a torx screw son hopefully I'll be able to remove this to sharpen it in future?

Overall, this is a trimmer that I really wanted to like, based on some very smart decisions around the design of the product. It's best suited to people with lighter beards though.

Pros

- For everything provided, it doesn't take a lot of space or weigh a lot. You can also leave behind items that you don't find useful to make an even leaner kit.

Cons

My husband’s beard seems to sprout overnight. Give him twelve hours and he goes from clean-shaven to minor lumberjack. I bought this trimmer in the hope of keeping him recognisable. The 6,600 rpm motor purrs rather than roars, yet it ploughs through bristles without fuss. A full charge on its lithium battery lasts about three hours, and a quick USB-C top-up gets it ready for round two.

The bathroom in our house is more paddling pool than powder room thanks to two splash-happy children, so the IPX7 rating is handy. Trimmer, dock and heads all survive the daily deluge. The magnetic dock is oddly satisfying, each attachment snapping into place and staying there instead of wandering into the chaotic drawer with rogue hair ties.

When it comes to actual cutting, it does the job well. The colour-coded combs let me grab the right length even while half asleep after a night shift. It never tugs at the thicker patches under his chin, and the little foil shaver tidies his neckline neatly. My biggest gripe is that the shortest guard still leaves a smidgen more than classic designer stubble, so a steady hand is needed for a really tight look. The bright LED display is clear enough but insists on resetting to speed one after every switch-off, a minor irritation that somehow manages to feel bigger than it is.

Build quality feels better than the price suggests, the waterproof dock keeps stray hairs out of the sink, and the battery’s endurance means fewer frantic hunts for a charger. Give me a finer guard and a memory for my preferred speed setting, and I would call the whole package just about perfect.

This grey trimmer has been squatting on the edge of my basin for two weeks and it already behaves as though the bathroom is its own private salon. Pick it up and the cold, almost aluminium feel hints at decent build quality, but the real surprise sits under the hood. The motor spins at a claimed 6,600 rpm and, judging by how fast it chews through several days of beard growth, that claim might even be modest.

I charged it once on the day it arrived and, after a fortnight of stop-start grooming, the battery indicator is still sitting stubbornly above halfway. The magnetic heads pop on with a crisp click, and the coloured combs finally spare me the usual Russian roulette of guessing which guard I grabbed last time.

Performance is the headline. Beard, chest and the occasional rebellious eyebrow hair vanish without a single tug or pinch. Rinsing is no drama either, as the IPX7 seal lets me run it straight under the tap. It does raise the volume a notch when water gets involved, but nothing that would wake the household.

It is not perfect. The comb plastic feels a little flimsy, the charging cradle swallows more shelf space than I care to surrender, and the so-called “short” guard sits slightly longer than a traditional number two. Anyone chasing five-o-clock-shadow stubble might grumble.

Even so, the combination of oomph, marathon battery life and those fuss-free magnetic heads makes this a real step up from my battered old clipper. I can live with the bulky dock in exchange for the welcome order it brings to my morning routine.

Awesome! Stop looking. It says the battery lasts for months or whatever, but after shaving whole head, face, trimming nose and ears, it's down to 70% from 100% so I doubt that, but otherwise it's great. Swapping out the different heads is very pleasing and easy. Just magnets into place.  Love it.
Great value for the money.

I couldn’t wait to try this trimmer set, so naturally I tore into the box like a kid on Christmas morning. First impression: lots of attachments. You’ve got your standard guards, an adjustable one, a shaver head… and one attachment that looks suspiciously like it was designed to pull a tracking device out of your sinuses à la Total Recall. If you know, you know.

First thing: charge it up. The stand is a little flimsy—not “cheap plastic disaster,” but more “don’t aggressively bump this at 6 a.m.” flimsy. That said, it has slots for everything and magnets that actually hold the attachments in place. Bonus points for the fact that you can plug the stand into the wall, so whenever the trimmer is docked, it’s charging. That’s a genuinely nice touch and saves you from the dreaded mid-shave power death.

To turn it on, you have to hold the button for three seconds while it counts down like it’s launching a rocket. Once unlocked, you can cycle through multiple power levels with a click. The large, easy-to-read display is great—it shows battery percentage and other info so you don’t start shaving only to have it die halfway through your face. (Though again, dock it and this shouldn’t be an issue.)

Now for the real test. I had about four days of beard growth—too much for a normal razor. Usually I trim it down first, then go back with a traditional razor and shaving cream. This time, however, I decided to try the included shaver attachment.

Let’s be honest: you get what you pay for. This is not a fancy Norelco wet/dry rotary shaver. It did chew my face up a bit. But in fairness, I have very soft, baby-like skin that the ladies just absolutely adore. So that one’s on me. I tried it so you don’t have to. You’re welcome.

The magnetic heads work really well. They snap on securely and don’t come off during use—you actually have to give them a little tug to remove them. That’s reassuring when you’re shaving sensitive areas and don’t want surprise detachment incidents.

There are also a couple of angled attachments designed for hard-to-reach places… which we will not be discussing further here. Just know they exist, and they do what they’re supposed to do.

All in all, for the price, this is a very good deal. It’s day one, but it did its job. Even if that job briefly involved roughing up my face a little, that was my choice. I regret nothing.

Would recommend—just maybe don’t go full send with the shaver head on your first try.

Since I've noticed that sometimes sellers will sell different products on the same page, my review is for the "Beard Trimmer for Men, Magnetic Snap-on Replacement Heads & Charging Dock, IPX7 Waterproof Cordless Hair Clippers, Mens Grooming Kits for Mustache/Body/Face/Ear/Nose, Gray"

The item is sold in an unsealed black manufacturer's box. For hygienic items, my preference is that they are either lid sealed or completely sealed box and this was neither. That being said, I opened the box and the items in the box appeared to be unused and it was brand new.

I have my fair share of hair trimmers, some better than others so I decided to give this trimmer a try.

I like that this hair trimmer has the attachments via magnetic snap-on which I find unusual (and positive) because my other hair trimmers require snapping in and snapping off the different attachments. I also like that it not only trims facial hair but has attachment for nose hair or ear hair. I do like that it includes a charging dock. Most of my other trimmers charge by just plugging the charging cable into the trimmer itself and having a charging dock makes it more organized.

I also like that the hair trimmer has a digital numeric battery charge level indicator since I don't charge my hair trimmers after each use and with those hair trimmers that don't have any battery level shown, it has happened to me where the trimmer would stop when I wasn't finished using it. For those that do have digital numeric displays of the battery level, I generally recharge them when they get below 50%.

I also like that it charges via USB and not a proprietary cable (one of my trimmers use a proprietary charging cable and if this cable is lost, the trimmer becomes useless when the battery needs charging). This TRIMX trimmer doesn't suffer from this.

It has a waterproof or water resistant rating but I don't see myself using this in the shower or using it when it is wet since I don't consider it to be safe. If this is important to you that you can use it in the shower, it does appear to be able to do this.

I like all the different attachments like beard trimmer, hair trimmer, foil shaver, nose & ear trimmer, and body hair trimmer. I also like the different "safety" combs provided in the different sizes.

This is a very good product.

I picked up this TrimX hair trimmer to replace my husband’s previous beard trimmer that no longer worked, and I’m really pleased with the whole set. This particular package will take care of just about all your body hair needs – hair, beard, nose hair, body hair, you name it!

The main unit works really well, with a single on/off button controlling the two primary modes (low and high) and a display that shows the battery level. The best feature of this 5 in 1 grooming tool is that it uses magnetic heads to swap on the unit depending on what your needs are. There are attachments for beard trimming, hair trimming, body hair, nose and ear hair, and an electric shaver for a close shave of your face. There are also different sized attachments for the hair trimmer, so you can trim your hair longer or shorter depending on what you like. All of these attachments are water proof and easily rinsible to get them clean.

The set also comes with a charging base for you to station your trimmer when not in use. This base does take up quite a bit of space, so if you have a smaller counter on top of your vanity in the bathroom, you may choose not to set up the base and just charge with the cord.

The battery lasts quite a long time as well. It’s recommended that you fully discharge the battery for the first 6 months of use before you charge it up again, but the battery should last for about 2 hours of use, which is really a long time when you think about using it for just 5-10 minutes at a time.

The whole package comes with everything you’ll need to maintain this set, including a small brush to clean attachments and oil for the main blades. It’s recommended that you use a drop or two of oil on the blades every 6 months or so to maintain them. Considering you get a whole bottle of the stuff, I feel confident that this whole unit is going to last me for years to come.

Overall, I’m feeling really satisfied with the quality and value of this set. It provided a comfortable and close shave and is very useful to have on hand. Would order again.

This is a powerful, well-made grooming kit that delivers on performance, comfort, and convenience. A great addition to any man’s daily routine.

This trimmer impressed me right out of the box. The motor is strong, steady, and doesn’t bog down even on thicker hair. At 6,600 RPM, it delivers a smooth cut every time. The battery life is excellent — I easily get multiple weeks on a single charge, and the USB-C makes topping it off simple.

The magnetic snap-on heads are a standout feature. They connect cleanly and securely without fiddling around. Switching between beard, body, and nose trimming is fast, and each attachment fits with a solid, confident click. The color-coded combs are another smart touch; lengths are instantly recognizable, and the combs glide across the skin without pulling.

The magnetic dock is well-designed and keeps everything organized. It’s compact, stable, and keeps the trimmer and heads aligned without clutter. It also handles water splashes with no issue, which is great since the trimmer itself is fully waterproof. Using it in the shower or rinsing it off is hassle-free.

The nose trimmer and body-grooming blades work better than I expected. The 360° open-frame design trims cleanly from every angle without tugging, and the body blade is comfortable even on more sensitive areas. The grip feels secure, and the whole trimmer has a solid, high-quality build.

So many options to cut with. The battery came pre-charged and I still haven't had to charge it. Cuts hair just like a professional unit. This is nice razor, a great gift. I love the way the heads connect. No complaints. Worth the price.

Cut Quality on the Clippers Attachment is high tier; the blade design keeps standoff distance making it easy to bulk remove hair no matter how coarse it is without a single nick or irritation.

As for the Trimmer Attachment, I wish it was "Zero Gapped", it seems to have the same standoff distance as the clippers, if they changed that or came out with another attachment it would be about the best all in one trimmer on the market especially the value for the dollar.

Without the trimmer attachment being "Zero Gapped", the foil shaver attachment has a hard time with the short coarse hair left behind, however no problem with fine hairs.

It works great with pleasantly quiet operation, with a good weight.

I couldn’t be happier with this Beard Trimmer for Men. The magnetic snap-on replacement heads are a total game changer, switching between beard, body, and detail trimming takes literally a second. The charging dock keeps everything organized and always ready to go, and the battery life is excellent.

The trimming performance is super smooth and precise, with no pulling or uneven patches. The motor is powerful but surprisingly quiet. I also love that it’s fully waterproof, making it easy to rinse clean after every use.

Whether I’m shaping my beard, cleaning up edges, trimming my mustache, or tackling nose hair, this kit handles it all. The gray finish even looks great on the counter
