Update 12/25/25: they've sent me another "variant" of this product so I'm happy to review the new one.

First off, I see that the grey color is still $30 which keeps the 5-star value. At this writing the "blue" color is $50 which would drop it to 4 stars as less of a great deal.

But what impresses me is that the company has seemingly taken my suggestion for this latest variant, including a drawstring bag for accessories!  I specifically requested this and the new variant includes it! And the accessories fit in it, barely, which to me is a huge improvement over just the rack, which would work in a world without kids, cats, other humans, and knocking into things, but which I don't have space on a counter for anyhow.  Though they still include it. They also include a neck bib to catch falling hair.

This is really more than a beard trimming system, it would suffice for whole-head, whole-body barbering, and at 30 bucks with integrated lithium power, this is hard to beat. So a continued 5 stars, but a HIGHER 5 stars for the new variant!

Recommended.
________________________________________________
First, I'll note that as of this review, this item is a full 40% off, for a total price of $29.99, which I think it notable and a good deal. These comments will be based on that price. At the full $50 price I'd rate it as a 3 or 4 star item due to value considerations.  But at $30 it gest 5.

The trimmer fits the hand nicely and takes a charge readily either from a standard USBC charge cable, or the included charging rack. It takes 3 seconds of pressing to turn it on or off, which for me is slightly excessive as I have arthritis, would have preferred half the time. It counts the seconds for you on the display and it does feel like a long time when your thumb joint is hurting... it's a small button.

Unless a person is a heck of a lot more organized nor non-materialist than I am, they won't have space on the counter to set up the display stand so all the little attachments, only a few of which you'll probably ever use, are constantly taking up counter space and can be knocked off easily. I doubt I'll use the charging tray, or that you will either. Rather, I'd suggest a drawstring bag for the accessories, which is not included, but which makes more sense for throwing in a drawer and finding the things if you ever do use them.

I really like the design.. the magnetic catch of the cutting heads, and the easy-clipping color-coded trim heads for various depths of cut. As little hair as I have, this will be used not just on my beard, but sideburns and neck and much of my head. The little "shaver" attachment with a rotary whisker-shaver inside worked very well indeed to quickly get rid of the hairs between my eyebrows... hey, bushy eyebrows are a sign of character in the elderly, but a unibrow isn't the look to go for.

At the 30 buck price point I have no trouble giving this 5 stars. It doesn't come with a power brick, but what does these days... USBC is a reasonable way to go.

I'd recommend this product losing the custom charging rack and instead supplying a drawstring bag, which would allow them to lower the price to $25 and make it a standout.

Even so, this has my recommendation.

This set is really nice. It arrived charged - the battery was at 74% - so my husband could and did use it right away. He trimmed his beard and shaved around it. It trimmed it perfectly and the shaved parts of his face are so smoothe. He also tested out some of the other attachments, and they worked great. It comes with so many different attachments that he doesn't need any of his other devices anymore. That saves counter and storage space. It's very well made and sturdy. It handles well - fitting nicely in his hand. It was easy to use. The magnetic aspect of it is awesome. The automatic docking worked perfectly. The guards are held in place very securely. You'll marvel at everything that is included in this kit.  There are so many attachments, combs, shavers that I'm wondering how they did it all at this price point! For all that this comes with and the overall performance, I would definitely recommend this set. For the relatively price, it gives you everything you could ask for and more!

This Trimx beard trimmer is a great value.  Some time ago I stopped using a razor for my face and switched to using a beard trimmer. This trimmer kit is packed to the brim!

I generally avoid these types of trimmer kits because they seem to offer everything except top-notch shaving performance.

For just under $50 (at the time of this review), you get an incredible amount of value in the box. It begins with a waterproof electric razor equipped with a powerful 6600rpm motor. The razor includes a safety switch to prevent it from turning on during transit. To deactivate it, simply press your finger down on the power button for 3 seconds.

In my initial testing, the razor did an okay job of trimming my beard using one of the five beard attachments.  One thing I immediately miss from this razor is the ability to "dial" in my preferred cutting length, which is an option with more expensive trimmers.

Although I didn’t test this trimmer for its advertised 180-minute battery life, I used it extensively and was pleasantly surprised to find that the battery didn’t drain quickly.

And returning to my initial point, this is the issue I find: it comes with so many attachments, including a comb, a carrying pouch, and even a cape, that the trimmer itself becomes secondary. In fact, you even receive a docking station that not only charges your trimmer but also magnetically holds all the attachments.  It can trim your nose, it can trim your ears, and it can shave your body hair.

And, yes, the trimmer even has a digital display that gives you remaining charge time, running rpm, and a cleaning reminder.

As I sit here, I can’t help but think this is the ideal trimming set for a young man embarking on his journey into facial and body grooming. However, as an older man, I’m faced with a dilemma. I know that quality trimmers without all these attachments start low-end at this price point.  This raises concerns about the overall durability of the trimmer. I own a few expensive trimmers, and you can feel the difference in quality between them and this TRIMX offering.  It feels cheap.

I give kudos to TRIMX for packing so much stuff into the box -- including blade oil -- at an affordable price point.  Just remember, folks, you get what you pay for, and I would suspect this trimmer's lifespan will be short-lived.
Just received it. First shave, without any attachments, very smooth and close.  Nose attachment will take some trials to see how good it is (maybe I'll add the fifth star later).

If you're looking for a dedicated mustache trimmer, this is fantastic! The adjustable blade (0.5mm to 14mm) delivers incredibly precise cuts for mustache styling, sideburn shaping, and clean edge lines. The sharp Japanese steel blade glides smoothly without any tugging or discomfort.

I appreciate the USB charging flexibility and the LED battery display that prevents unexpected shutdowns. The IPX8 waterproof rating makes post-use cleanup effortless - just rinse and you're done. It's also impressively lightweight and quiet during operation.

My reason for 4 stars rather than 5: this isn't built for efficient full beard trimming. The slim blade design that makes it perfect for detail work becomes a limitation when tackling larger facial hair areas. Anyone wanting an all-in-one solution for both precision work and general beard maintenance should look elsewhere.

Bottom line: outstanding at what it's designed for - precision mustache and detail grooming. Just understand it's a specialist tool, not a jack-of-all-trades beard trimmer.

This trimmer excels at mustache and detail work! The precision blade with adjustable lengths (0.5mm to 14mm) is perfect for shaping my mustache, cleaning up sideburns, and edging. The Japanese steel blade is sharp and cuts cleanly without pulling.

The USB rechargeable feature is convenient, and the LED battery indicator ensures I never run out of power mid-trim. Being IPX8 waterproof means easy cleanup under running water. The lightweight, quiet design is comfortable for detailed work.

However, I'm giving it 4 stars instead of 5 because it's limited for full beard trimming. The narrow precision blade is great for detail work but not ideal for efficiently trimming larger beard areas. If you mainly need a versatile beard trimmer that handles both detailing and full coverage, this might feel too specialized.

I've had a beard for the vast majority of my adult life, yet I'm never had a mustache trimmer.  I'm always relied on scissors, now that I've tried this I don't want to go back.  I love the concept and the idea of keeping my mustache clean and the hairs even.  This trimmer was good in that it showed me what I want, even if it isn't the long term answer.

Issues: Plastic connections to all moving parts, will effect longevity.  The blades pop off really easy with slight front to back pressure, I set it down on counter and it flew off - took me over a minute to re-attach.

Pros: Waterproof and easy to clean.  Light weight and easy to move around.  Decent texture on handle for holding, even when wet.

I picked up this URBANER precision mustache trimmer because I needed something small and accurate for quick touch‑ups between full shaves. After using it regularly, here’s my honest experience:
What I Really Liked:

Precision is excellent. The Japanese steel blade really does make a difference—clean, sharp lines without snagging or pulling. Great for shaping a mustache, edging around the lips, or cleaning up small spots under the nose.
Very lightweight and easy to maneuver. It feels almost like holding a pen, which makes detailed trimming a lot easier compared to bulkier electric shavers.
Quiet and smooth. The motor isn’t loud or buzzy, which is nice for early morning grooming.
USB rechargeable. Convenient to charge anywhere, and the battery life has held up well for several grooming sessions.
IPX8 waterproof. I’ve rinsed the blade directly under water without issues—makes maintenance super simple.

What Could Be Better:

Not ideal for thicker beards. It’s meant for detailing, and it excels at that, but it struggles a little if I try to trim larger patches of dense hair.
No length guards included. Since it’s purely for precision, it trims very close—great for outlining but not for blending or trimming to a specific length.
The power button feels slightly small. Works fine, just could be more prominent.

I like this trimmer a lot because the head is small, perfect for mustache and also eyebrows. Grip is good and the attack angle allows accurate detailing with ease. Battery lasts a long time, several uses, before needing a recharge.

Would honestly have to say this trimmer is junk..... ability to cut a mustache is very poor.  I tried it, all it did was jam up and stop mid stroke.... the size is ok too bad it doesn't do as advertised i had hopes it would.
This is my 1st time using a mustache trimmer. I usually used manual. This is great hygiene tool. Love the quality, very portable. Battery last longer before another charge.

This trimmer feels well made and is comfortable when holding it. It's not too heavy which is nice and holds onto a charge pretty well. The piece that supposed to control the depth of the cut is kind of weird but so far it works. I can get clean lines when using this and so far I have had no issues with it slowing down due to being clogged with hair. I will say that I have a longer beard and only really use this razor for trimming around the edges but it works really well for me so far. I will update later on battery life and how sharp it stays.

I've used this URBANER Precision Mustache Trimmer a handful of times now, mostly for quick touch-ups on my mustache edges, sideburns, and keeping things neat under the nose. It works exactly as intended – no major surprises, no pulling or snagging on the hair (at least for my fairly average facial hair), and the cuts are clean enough for everyday grooming.
It definitely feels like a budget-friendly option: lightweight plastic build, simple design, nothing fancy or premium in the hand, but that's okay because the price reflects it. The USB rechargeable battery holds up fine for short sessions, the LED indicator is handy, and being IPX8 waterproof makes rinsing it under the tap super easy after use. The adjustable combs give decent length options for detailing without needing a bunch of extra attachments.
Not going to pretend it's as powerful or precise as my more expensive trimmer, but for the money, it's reliable for light, focused work and travels well. Makes a great low-key gift for a guy who wants something simple and effective for mustache/beard maintenance without spending a ton – think stocking stuffer or birthday for the bearded friend who doesn't need all the bells and whistles.
Would buy again if this one ever gives out. Solid value.
Cuts pretty good and the guard is adjustable which I love because I don’t have to have 5 or 6 guards all over the place. Battery held up good after using it for 10 min. Happy with it.

This trimmer is an excellent choice for anyone who needs a portable, low-profile grooming tool. Unlike most bulky trimmers, this one is slim and lightweight, making it a perfect fit for a travel kit. It charges via USB-C, which is a huge plus, and it means you can use almost any universal cable to charge it on the go. While its light weight suggests a smaller battery, the convenience of USB-C makes up for it.

The trimmer performs well for its intended purpose: detailing and trimming. I wouldn't recommend it for tackling a full, thick beard, but for keeping a mustache or edges clean, it does the job efficiently. It also survived a light wetness test, so you can rinse it in the sink or use it in a damp environment without fear. My only minor gripe is that the included guards are non-standard sizes compared to typical US clippers, so it may take a little trial and error to find your preferred length. Overall, it's a budget-friendly tool for precision work.

I like this little trimmer. It's compact and easy to travel with. I really like the fact that it charges by USB-C. Makes life a bit easier. But keep in mind that it is not compatible with fast charging. In other words you need to use a USB-A to C cable to charge this. A little annoying but it is what it is.

You won't get a super close shave with this. Definitely won't replace your razor, but it will keep edges nice and tidy. My razor won't reach the spot right below my nostrils and this will so it's earned it's place in my bathroom because of it.

It's waterproof which is nice. Can use it to trim intimate areas if you're so inclined. Although... it'd feel a little weird to use it there and your face. But you do you.

Feels pretty solid in the hand. Whole thing is plastic though.

This trimmer was a letdown. The biggest issue is that it pulls hair instead of cutting cleanly. After a few passes on my face, it tugged enough that I just stopped using it.

For something marketed as a precision detail trimmer, the blade design isn’t very precise. The cutting surfaces are fairly wide relative to the overall size, which makes it harder to do tight, accurate shaping. It doesn’t feel refined or sharp.

It’s also extremely single-purpose. There are no attachments, no versatility, and it can’t really be used for anything beyond very basic detail trimming, and even that it doesn’t do well.

For the price, this feels like junk. I expected much better performance from something advertised as a precision grooming tool.

I really like this trimmer.

I’m a mustache guy, so having a dedicated tool just for mustache work makes a big difference. A standard beard trimmer works fine for bulk trimming, but it’s not ideal when you’re trying to do detailed shaping and cleanup around the lip line.

What sets this apart is the narrow blade width. It allows for much more precise trimming and shaping, especially around the edges and under the nose. It’s easier to control and gives you cleaner lines without accidentally taking off more than you intended.

It’s lightweight, easy to handle, and being USB rechargeable is convenient. The waterproof design is also a plus for easy cleaning.

If you’re serious about keeping your mustache looking sharp, a smaller precision trimmer like this just makes sense. It’s a noticeable upgrade over trying to use a full-size beard trimmer for detailed work.

This is a nifty trimmer that's a step up from pencil trimmers that take a AAA battery. I had no issues getting a close trim. It's not smooth-skin close, but if you're traveling and rocking a 5 o'clock shadow look it's great. It didn't cut or pinch my skin during use.

The IPX8 waterproof rating is awesome for easy cleaning under the tap. The optional slider for different cut lengths is an ingenious feature if you're not going for a close trim. The blade comes completely off for cleaning, which makes maintenance straightforward.

At $32 at the time of writing, the price feels a little steep compared to other options given that it's a plastic body and parts except for the blade. That said, the packaging is really nice and does justify the price if you're buying this as a gift - it presents well right out of the box. The charge time is slow at 6 or more hours, and it also lacks USB Power Delivery (PD), so you must use a 5V/1A charger - which isn't included in the box. With all fast chargers in regular use these days, it's an inconvenient omission.

Overall, this is a solid travel trimmer with a standout waterproof rating and thoughtful design features. The price is easier to justify as a gift given the presentation, but as a personal purchase the charging limitations and cost are worth considering.

Very poor quality, very light weight and weak item. Its rechargeable but the blades dont cut well and it feels incredibly cheap. Theres no weight at all to this trimmer. It compares to a cheap nose hair trimmer that takes a single AAA battery in design and weight. Just not a good quality item and not worth the price. There are much better trimmers on the website, seek another.

I received the URBANER Precision Mustache Trimmer as part of the Amazon Vine Customer Review of Free Product Program, and after spending some time with it, I’d describe it as a mixed experience.
First, credit where it’s due: the presentation is excellent. The packaging feels thoughtful and well-designed, and everything is neatly arranged. In the box, you get the trimmer, adjustable guide comb, cleaning brush, USB charging cable, and clear instructions. It definitely gives a strong first impression and feels more premium than its price point might suggest.
In terms of performance, it does an adequate job on mustache and light detail work. The Japanese steel blade is sharp enough for edging and light trimming around the lip line and sideburns. It’s lightweight and easy to maneuver, which makes detail grooming fairly simple. The IPX8 waterproof rating is also a plus — being able to rinse the whole unit under water makes cleanup quick and convenient.
Where this trimmer falls short is in the overall build quality. While it looks sleek, the plastic body feels thin and somewhat hollow. The adjustable comb works, but it doesn’t inspire a lot of confidence and feels like it could wear out or loosen over time. The power is acceptable for fine detailing, but it struggles a bit with thicker or denser hair and may require multiple passes.
Battery charging via USB is convenient, though the charging time is fairly long compared to some other compact trimmers I’ve used.
Overall, this is a solid option for light grooming and travel use, especially if you’re mainly maintaining edges rather than doing heavy trimming. It looks better than it feels, and while it performs well enough for basic mustache detailing, the budget construction keeps it from being a standout. If durability and long-term sturdiness are priorities, you may want to consider other options, but for casual touch-ups, it gets the job done.

This trimmer works well and does exactly what it’s meant to do. It’s compact and easy to handle, which makes it especially convenient for travel or quick touch-ups. The precision blade helps keep lines clean and neat, and the included attachments add some flexibility for trimming different areas like the nose and ears.

I’m not a huge fan of needing to replace the battery instead of having a rechargeable option, but the upside is that it’s lightweight and simple to pack without worrying about charging cords. Performance has been consistent, and it provides a smooth trim without pulling or irritation.

Overall, it’s a practical grooming tool that gets the job done and is great to keep on hand for trips or everyday maintenance.

Sometimes, a guy just needs a trim. This trimmer from Urbaner fits the bill. Specifically, I got it for travel. Here's what I really like about it:

1. It's narrow and compact. The edge is just under an inch wide, so you can maneuver it in tight spaces. Japanese stainless steel stays sharp, cuts sharp, looks sharp.
2. It's adjustable in length, from .08"-0.55". Don't need multiple heads/guards traveling with you; it's all right there.
3. You get a 1-year warranty. I like people who back their products.
4. One of my faves: it's waterproof! Yes, I know. For precision beard trimming, most of us aren't doing it in the shower where we can't see and don't usually have mirrors. But if you're going to use this guy for manscaping, there you go. Plus, to clean it, just run it under water and you're good to go.
5. It comes with an ear/nose trimmer attachment. That's nice and easy-peasy.

Bottom line is that this trimmer is pretty much exactly what I wanted for travel. It works great, great value for the money, feels good in the hand without being too big or too small. It's a good one!

This is a functional mustache and beard trimmer. However, I do have particularly thick beard hair and there were times when this trimmer struggled a bit to cut it consistently. A few areas required second and third passes. It’s also a bit noisy. The size is perfect and the waterproof design makes it very convenient to use in the shower. Overall, I’m glad I have it. I just wish it had a slightly more powerful motor.

This trimmer seems to have a strong motor, but the cutting blades were not good enough.  In fact they had a tendency to pull on some of the hairs instead of cutting them. Ouch! My 12 year old Remington is on it's last legs, but it still cuts much better than this Urbaner.  $20 for a trimmer that hurts??  No, thanks!

This trimmer is compact but definitely packs a punch. It seems to be high quality and it fits well in the hand. It’s not too thick or thin and has a nice weight to it. The product itself is designed well and it is easy to switch your trimming mode. The trimmer its self is a great value because of the size and how well it cuts. It’s also not too too loud which is quite nice.

The 0.8-inch blade on this URBANER Mustache Trimmer is exactly what I needed for precision work. It's much easier to use than a full-size beard trimmer for shaping under the nose and around the lip. The swap-on nose and ear trimmer head works great too, making it a solid all-in-one grooming tool for the face. It feels well-built and handles the 'detail work' effortlessly.

I tried nose and ear trimmer attachment and could say that my Panasonic tool for <$10 works much better.
This one makes weird noises like it touches something inside.

Also, I used it to trim my eyebrows and found that regular hair clipper works better too.
Overall It feels flimsy, so I am not sure for how long you can use it before it gets broken.

What I really liked in this tool, it is adjustable clipper guard, so you don't need to have several ones with different sizes.

I think this tool makes sense for people who need all-in-one device but you will get a better quality if you have separate tool for each use case.

This mustache trimmer looks nice and feels good in hand. The size is compact and easy to control. I also like that it is waterproof so I can rinse it after use. The trimming performance is okay. It cuts evenly most of the time and the different length settings are helpful. The motor sounds strong and does not feel too weak. My only issue is with the quality. After a few uses it does not feel as solid as I expected. The attachment head feels a little loose and not very sturdy. It still works but I expected better build quality. Overall it does the job and has good features, but the quality could be better.

The URBANER mustache trimmer actually comes with a Duracell battery. Nice surprise. It works well and is much smaller than I expected. The razor blade head is compact and works great for detailing edges precisely. It includes an adjustable plastic guard that changes lengths, though I can't speak to its effectiveness since I don't need it. You can also swap the head for a nose trimmer attachment, which is handy as a two-in-one tool. Cleaning is easy. You can submerge the heads, and the razor head disassembles by popping off the housing to brush or rinse the blade.

Good value for an electric trimmer under $20, especially if you want a narrower blade or the convenience of a multi-grooming tool with the nose/ear attachment. Just note this leaves some stubble and won't give you a razor-close shave.

This trimmer includes a battery so that you can operate the device right away. This device is not rechargeable, and therefore does not include a charger. I appreciate how compact it is and how simple it is to swap the heads and replace the batteries. My boyfriend uses it to fix the lines on his beard and mustache as it grows out. I don't believe this tool would be the best tool to accomplish a close shave or a full scale trim of the facial hair. Delicate touches and on-the-go functionality are the strengths of this trimmer, not power or size. It's great value for the money, and I hope that the waterproof protection grants it more durability to make this an even better buy.

I initially viewed this as a potential travel shaver for use in an overnight bag given its small size; the usable blade is more like 0.65", making it painfully slow for daily use on a full face. But it did a lousy job trimming in general, cutting less than half the stubble in its first pass while also pulling on multiple hairs. Ouch! It's also not waterproof and the comb's build quality won't hold up for long. Just do yourself a favor and buy the Norelco OneBlade, which costs twice as much but is VASTLY superior.

I really like that you get a mustache trimmer as well as the extra attachments for nose hair and ears. The device itself is easy to use, just pop the battery in, put on the right trimmer head, and turn it on. You can use this to trim beard, mustache, sideburns, nose hairs and those annoying ear hairs. One thing to note, when using any of the attachments, be sure to blow them out or remove and clean, especially the nose hair one. If hair gets stuck in it, it can cause the motor to stop in the middle of cutting which could be painful if it is pulling on a nose hair at the time. A small cleaning brush would be a very worthwhile accessory for this (hence only 4 stars) as it really should be cleaned fully after each use.

The Vacuum Beard Trimmer for Men is a versatile grooming tool designed for precision and convenience, particularly suited for men seeking a clean, hassle-free grooming experience. Here's a detailed breakdown of its key features and performance:

Design and Build

This trimmer has a sleek, ergonomic design, making it comfortable to hold and maneuver. The trimmer’s waterproof feature adds a layer of convenience, allowing it to be rinsed under running water for easy cleaning. The adjustable length settings, paired with two comb attachments, give users flexibility to trim beards, mustaches, and stubble to their preferred lengths. It’s also lightweight, making it easy to carry for travel.

Vacuum Technology

One standout feature is the integrated vacuum system. It efficiently captures trimmed hair, reducing the mess typically associated with beard trimming. This is especially useful for quick grooming sessions without having to worry about cleaning up afterward. While the vacuum feature performs decently, it may not capture 100% of the hair, particularly when trimming thicker beards. However, it still minimizes mess to a great extent compared to traditional trimmers.

Blades and Performance

The high-precision blades are designed for skin-friendliness, ensuring a smooth, tug-free experience even on sensitive skin. The blades stay sharp for longer, providing consistent cutting performance over time. The trimmer works well on various hair types and lengths, from thicker, coarse beards to finer stubble.

Adjustable Length Settings

With adjustable length settings and two comb attachments, this trimmer allows for precise control over the trimming length. It offers a wide range of lengths, making it ideal for men who prefer to change up their beard styles, from clean stubble to fuller beards. The adjustability ensures even trimming without needing multiple tools.

Battery Life and Charging

Battery life is respectable, providing enough power for multiple trimming sessions on a single charge. A full charge typically lasts up to 60 minutes of continuous use, making it ideal for routine grooming. The charging process is straightforward, and some models may offer USB charging for added convenience.

Waterproof and Easy Maintenance

One of the major selling points is its waterproof design. This makes the trimmer not only easy to clean but also suitable for use in wet conditions, such as in the shower. The detachable components rinse easily under water, maintaining the trimmer’s hygiene with minimal effort.

I had been using an older model Panasonic ear/nose-hair trimmer for over five years until it finally gave up the ghost. This one is even better. In the past two months, it stopped working four times. I puzzled over what to do and considered ditching it. But since it's a nice product and there isn't an obvious replacement to buy, I decided to experiment a bit. The first time it started working again after I removed the battery and reinserted it. The second time this didn't work but rapping the base of the trimmer on a countertop got it working again. The third time, when I hadn't used it for over a week, neither of these solutions worked and I was resigned to ditching it but decided to bank it harder against the countertop and after 5-6 hits it started working again. The fourth time, when I hadn't used for several days, 2 knocks did the trick. It's been working perfectly for about four months since then.

I'm mentioning this because other reviews also mentioned this problem (though not the potential solutions), so it may affect your purchase decision and, if you purchase this trimmer, you may need to deal with the problem.  I puzzled over what caused the problem and two guesses are that it is either (1) a loose connection between the battery and motor due to low manufacturing tolerances or (2) the lubricant hardens during periods when the trimmer isn't used. But I'm not sure.

I have three recommendations. First, consider another trimmer that doesn't have this problem (or worse problems). Unfortunately, I don't recall seeing any. Second, check out the product carefully during the return window so you can exchange it for one that (hopefully) doesn't have this problem. Third, if it's past the return period, try using it regularly and try these solutions before you ditch it.

Updated Review: I had to drop it to 4 stars from 5, as the on/off switch drives me insane. It's a terrible, terrible design. Everything else works as described below.

Original Review:
It’s true that as I’ve gotten a little older, my nose hairs have taken on a life of their own. I had a nose trimmer for many years, and it was amazing, but I accidentally left it in a hotel while traveling. I’m sure the housekeepers were thrilled to find it on the sink. I bought another trimmer, but it felt like someone was taking pliers and yanking the hairs from my nose. Then, I found the ConairMan Nose Hair Trimmer during my search for the best nose hair trimmers. I’ve had it for a couple of weeks, and I’m impressed.

The big thing I find lacking with most trimmers is their ability to cut close to the skin without nicking or pulling. This trimmer is amazing, and it does as great of a job as my older trimmer, which is lost to history. Not only does it truly get close to the skin (meaning no stray hairs), but it doesn’t hurt while trimming. It does a great job at trimming those crazy stray hairs with ease. The trimmer comes with a nice carrying case for travel, and I hope I don’t leave this one at a hotel to surprise another housekeeper.

The only issue with the Conair is the on/off switch. You turn the battery door slightly to turn the trimmer off and on. The problem is if you turn it a bit too far, the battery door comes off in your hand. It was truly a poor design choice by Conair, and the switch is my only complaint about the trimmer. Honestly, it’s annoying when it happens, but once I start trimming, that complaint fades because of how well the trimmer performs. Should I give it 4/5 stars? Maybe it’s because the on/off switch is terrible, but I bought it for the trimming function, and that part works flawlessly.

Pros: It is a great little compact trimmer that comes with a nice carrying case. It does a great job of cutting close to the skin without pulling hairs and nicking the skin.
Cons: The on/off switch placement stinks. It’s terrible, but the rest of the trimmer is perfect.

I recently picked up the Philips Norelco Nose Trimmer 5000 (NT5600/62), and it’s quickly become my go-to tool for keeping nose hairs under control. For anyone who hates the bulky, awkward feel of some trimmers — this one hits the sweet spot: it’s wonderfully lightweight, easy to maneuver, and most importantly, completely painless and gentle; you hardly feel anything even when trimming deep inside.

The design and feel stand out right away. It’s compact in the hand, so you don’t have that panicky “am I poking my brain?” sensation that some heavier trimmers give. That alone makes grooming sessions much easier and more comfortable — less second-guessing whether you’re being too aggressive or too hesitant. The shape and weight make it easy to steer just where you need.

I appreciate the different trimming heads included in the kit — meant for nose, ears, and even eyebrows — though in practice I mostly use the standard head for nostril trimming because it’s simple, quick, and does a clean job. The versatility is useful if I need a quick tidy-up elsewhere, but for everyday use, one head is all I really need.

Battery life has also been solid. I don’t feel like I’m constantly having to swap batteries, which is a major plus: the trimmer stays reliably powered, and I’ve never had the frustration of a mid-trim battery die mid-use. For a product at this price point, that’s impressive — and it makes the entire tool feel more premium than I expected.

That’s when the Philips Norelco 5000 Nose Trimmer enters your life.

The Ear Hair Situation

Everyday Use

I would give the Winsea Ear and Nose Hair Trimmer for Men and Women zero stars, but the system cruelly insists I give it one. Think of that star not as praise, but as a small memorial candle for the brief life of this trimmer.

When it first arrived, I was optimistic. It looked sleek. It hummed with the quiet confidence of a tiny barber ready to tame the dark forest within my nostrils. I used it once. Once. And for that single glorious moment, it performed its duty. Nose hair trimmed. Civilization preserved. I thought, “Finally, technology has caught up with the human nose.”

Fast forward one month.

I have a date tonight.

A real one. The kind where you sit across from another human being who can see your face from three feet away. So naturally, I go to prepare. Shower. Shirt ironed. Confidence building.

Then I open the drawer.

I pull out the Winsea Nose Hair Trimmer, my former ally in the war against visible nostril shrubbery. I press the power button.

Nothing.

No hum. No whirr. No brave little motor charging into battle.

Just silence.

Now here’s the kicker: it’s rechargeable. I plug it in. The little charging light comes on like it’s doing something important. I wait. I give it time. I plug it in again. I move outlets like a desperate electrician trying to resurrect a toaster.

Still nothing.

This thing has the operational lifespan of a fruit fly with burnout.

So now here I am, 45 minutes before a date, staring into the mirror like a man betrayed by modern technology. My nostrils look like they’re preparing for winter. If the wind hits the right direction, a small bird could attempt nesting.

Thank you, Winsea. Truly. I bought a grooming tool and instead received what appears to be a one-time-use ceremonial device. Apparently the battery is rechargeable, but the motor itself has chosen early retirement.

For the price of this trimmer I received one successful nose haircut and a lifetime supply of distrust.

If anyone needs me tonight, I’ll be at dinner carefully angling my head so the candlelight doesn’t illuminate what is now clearly a nasal ecosystem.

Would not recommend unless you enjoy disposable electronics or plan to trim your nose hair exactly once in your lifetime.

It’s easy to use, delivers great results, and I’m very satisfied with it. Highly recommend!

"I was eagerly waiting to get my hands on this product to try it out, and I must say that it has not disappointed me at all! It works perfectly, removing every unwanted hair gently but firmly at the same time. It has several easy-to-use accessories, and I highly recommend it for its versatility. Order yours now!

This nose hair trimmer works perfectly and is very easy to use. It trims quickly and painlessly, leaving a clean and neat result. I really like that it’s rechargeable and comes with multiple attachments for nose, ears, eyebrows, and facial hair. The design feels sturdy and high quality. Excellent value for the price—highly recommended!”

I used a Panasonic nose hair trimmer for about 5 years until it finally gave out. I decided to try this Setonia trimmer as a replacement, and I’m very impressed. The blades are much sharper, so it cuts much more cleanly and efficiently than my old one. I also love that it’s rechargeable—it’s so much more convenient than dealing with batteries. Definitely a great upgrade!

This product is the best nose, eyebrow and ear hair trimmer that I have ever purchased.  I have bought at least ten similar items over the past 20+ years, and none of them worked as promoted.  I highly recommend this product.

The Setonia trimmer is quick, and easy to use. It trims nose and ear hair efficiently without pulling, and the rechargeable feature is super convenient. Compact, quiet, and easy to clean .perfect for home or travel. Highly recommend
Satisfied and works great. They are also a good gift for someone.
work very will
Ordered this trimmer.  It does exactly what is say it does.  Painless, clean and quick.  Would buy again!
I chose this trimmer because of the USB type C. I was pleasantly surprised by the compact size. It works silently, shaves the hairs well, it's nice to hold in your hand.
Easily charged via USB C, pen sized, and has a nice little led ring near the tip. Metal casing and chrome accents on both ends love it.

Battery lasts for weeks between charges. No faults. Nothing but reliable service. Use in wet everyday and wash out under faucet. Doubt I use it as instructed but it is the Energizer bunny of shavers. It does what I expected for the cost. Consistent, accurate, reliable, never cut my skin or pulled a hair, can’t say the same of other and more expensive shavers.

Use this for the hair between the ears on my dogs feet as the hair gets super long and makes them slide on hardwood. Works amazing and doesn’t scare them. Battery life lasts for 4-5 uses and removes the hair super easy without nicking them at all.

(tldr at end) I bought this trimmer ~6 mo ago and so far it has been handling on great. Trims my hair good, works good on the lawn, and I have not needed to replace the trimmer blades yet, so I can only assume they're nice and made well, but that does not mean I do not have any nitpicks.

The good:
Blade is durable and sharp, has not failed to cut my hair at all. Its somewhat large though, so be mindful, but I havent had any issues with that.

By far the best grooming product I've ever purchased. I've owned a set of Wahl's hair clippers but assumed the grooming set would be just as good. Extremely wrong, horrible purchase and total loss of money as they understandably don't accept returns once it's used.

This is a great design, quiet and smooth, cuts well, easy to clean and maintain, comes with various sizes, a nice design box, rechargeable, waterproof. I've had it for about a year or 2 now and it's a game changer for just trims instead of always shaving and ending up with dull blades so quickly.
Works surprisingly well, trims cleanly without pulling, and the battery lasts long enough for several grooming sessions.

It’s a good product but compared to the Manscape, it’s a less quality shaver. It’s inexpensive and affordable and leaves a close, fine stubble. The attachments are flimsy and a bit annoying to get on and off. Battery life seems great.

For overall build, quality and design, it needs some improvements. The plastic body is very made of very thin plastic and is very smooth. It easily falls out of my hands and has no textured grooves to help with grip. I’m surprised it didn’t break when it hit the tub but I’m certain it won’t take repeated drops. Using the shaver in the shower is almost pointless. When wet, this shaver does almost nothing.

Good, inexpensive product. Needs improvement. Be prepared to buy it again sooner than you’d think.

I used to approach grooming my man bits the way people approach haunted houses... Slowly, nervously, and prepared for much pain.
Then I met the Manspot Manscape trimmer. (Insert heavenly harp sound here)
This thing doesn’t just trim.  It handles the delicate zones with the precision of a surgeon, and the confidence of a superhero. No snagging. No surprises.
Now everything glides smoothly across my nether region like a luxury sports car on freshly paved asphalt. It’s not just clean, it’s a premium love zone. The kind of smooth that makes you stand taller, walk prouder, and reconsider every cheap trimmer you ever trusted.
Honestly, I didn’t just upgrade my grooming routine.
I upgraded the neighborhood.
If your current trimmer treats your weiner like a lawn mower treats weeds, do yourself a favor and switch.

Five stars. Zero fear. Maximum confidence.

The cut quality on this trimmer is excellent — smooth, even, and comfortable without pulling or irritation. It’s very easy to use, even for first-timers, and the design makes it simple to maneuver and control.
The size is perfect: compact but solid, making it highly portable and easy to pack for travel. It feels durable and well-built, not flimsy or cheap, which adds confidence during use. Battery life has been reliable, and maintenance is quick and straightforward.For the value for money, this is an easy win. It performs exactly as advertised and delivers consistent results. And yes — my wife definitely loves that I got this and keeps joking she can’t wait for “play time,” which makes the five-star rating even easier 😄. Overall, a great grooming tool that checks all the boxes.

I’ve been really impressed with this trimmer so far. It gives a close, comfortable trim with no tugging or nicks, even on more sensitive areas, and the ceramic blade feels much smoother than the cheap metal ones I’ve used before. The different guard sizes make it easy to switch between grooming my groin, chest, and underarms without worrying about going too short. I also like that it’s waterproof, so I can use it in the shower and rinse it clean quickly when I’m done. The battery life is excellent—I can get multiple full-body trims on a single charge, and the USB‑C charging is convenient. Overall, it feels sturdy in the hand, runs quietly, and makes grooming way less stressful; I’d definitely recommend it to anyone looking for an easy, hassle‑free body trimmer.

This shaver comes with the 1.5 mm comb attached. Was charged right out of the box, so I gave it a try on my very long upper leg hair. Pfft got maybe 50% of it, and that required going back and forth several times over the same area from several directions. Before sending it back I removed the comb entirely and tried it. WORKS GREAT! For years I've been using a Wahl clipper made for head haircuts, bought during covid, that would shave the leg and other hair cleanly but nick a lot since the end was sharp, made for head haircuts. Well without the comb this baby shaves just as close and as fast! I can put by finger right on the blade tip while it is running and no cuts at all. The tips are rounded metal, with the ceramic blade slightly below them.

This shaver doesn't come with a charger, just a USB cable with an old-style USB-A plug on the end and a note on the cord saying "only use a 5V adapter, voltages over 5V will NOT charge the device". Big eye roll here. Right, a 5V USB-A wall adapter in 2026. Hmmm. Didn't my old Galaxy S8+ phone from 2018 use those? I dug through the years-old phone stuff and yep, there was one, 5V 1A USB-A that I bought at a grocery store way back then. Works! :) Well for $34 or whatever this body hair shaver was I would say it is a great deal. I really don't understand the whole comb thing for body hair anyway. So you *want* your body hair to be a certain length and not be clean shaven? Why? Maybe for chest hair? I guess I'm just not with the "barbershop on every corner" times now. :)

All-in-all: Recommended! Buy this! Buy one for a friend! A+++

I bought this to replace my older bulkier trimmer which has a failing battery.  I use this trimmer on my face and body (including male groin) - works great!

THINGS I LIKE ABOUT IT:
- Does not knick even the most sensitive, thin-skinned parts of the male groin.
- compact size - great for travel!
- Can be used wet/dry.  I have not used it in the shower but it's good to know I can blast it with water liberally to clean it.
- This unit doesn't seem to throw hair clippings all over the place like my old trimmers did.  I seriously had to wipe down the entire bathroom counter/sink/faucte after using my old clippers, which was annoying.  With this unit, all my facial hair trimmings fall neatly into the sink I'm trimming over.
- Long battery life, so no need to the bring charging cable when traveling (for trips less than 2 weeks I just charge it and my electric toothbrush and don't bother bringing either of their chargers).
- Nice semi-grippy surface material - feels nice holding it, wet or dry.  Overall has a solid, high-quality feel to it.
- I suspect the ceramic blades will hold up longer than traditional steel blades.  Past steel-blade trimmers Ive owned always seemed to corrode after enough time (this probably could be prevented with regular re-oiling as recommended, but who actually does that regularly, aside from professional barbers with high-end trimmers.)

THINGS I DON'T LIKE ABOUT IT:
- There doesn't seem to be a "button lock" for travel.  I had this in my toiletry bag within in my carry-on backpack, and while waiting at the gate for our flight my girlfriend heard a faint buzzing coming from my backpack.  If she hadn't heard it, I wouldn't have dug through my bag to turn it off, which would have resulted in it depleting its battery well before we landed at our destination airport hours later.  This would have sucked since I didn't pack the charge cable and I needed to be freshly trimmed up for an event we were going to during our trip.  I will have to come up with something to keep the button from getting pressed when packed for future travel.  I knocked a star off because of this - if it had way to lock the button when traveling I would give it 5 stars.
- There is no full blade cover included.  Sometimes the inside of my toiletry bag can get gunky, so it would have been nice for them to include a 2 cent piece of plastic that would snap on the same way as the guards, but would fully cover the blades to both keep them clean and to physically protect them.  I just put one of the included blade guards on when traveling, and this is probably plenty of protection, so no big deal.

Nicked me, YOUCH!  Overall pretty good, it's not a super smooth cut but it works well for all over.  The guards are nice but it included 2x of the same.  Not really sure what the one circular attachment is.  Battery life is good, used it for 30+ min and didn't die.

I love these! I’m a mother of 3 boys: 15, 12 and 9. I’ve purchased 2 of these for my oldest 2 boys which already have pubic hair and this does a great job a trimming that area. It’s very safe for them to use and gets the job done. I will get one for my other son when he gets there.

I love that is wireless, super lightweight and small enough to get in those hard to reach areas. I will keep purchasing this when it’s time to replace.

Parents, if you looking for a great quality, safe trimmer for your boys, this is the one!

Great Trimmer! It's light, sleek, easy to use. The guards slip on and off easily to change the hair trim length. The size and weight is great for travel. But my main complaint is that it has a Custom Charging port which is terrible. I hate when companies do this, because you have to keep this special stupid cord. Instead of just throwing it in my bag, I have to make sure that I have the special cord. The really obnoxious part is that it is a usb end to plug into your own charger. But that is the only negative. I found the trim quality was great. It is clean and smooth and can be used on any part you want to trim :)

This trimmer works really well! It feels good to hold and has a nice grip. It’s gentle on the skin with no pulling or cuts. I like that it’s waterproof, so I can use it in the shower and rinse it off after.

It’s an upgrade from my old trimmer that didn’t have USB charging. This one does, and it’s much easier to charge. The battery lasts a long time, and the charging base is simple to use. I feel safe using it, even on sensitive areas. It’s also nice that I can use a nose hair trimmer with it. Great trimmer for the price!

I’m super impressed with this trimmer! It cut my kid’s side hairline really sharp and clean without snagging at all. The motor feels powerful but the sound is surprisingly quiet, which kept my kid calm the whole time. The battery life is solid, and the grip feels comfortable so it’s easy to handle even for detailed trimming. Overall, it’s high quality, performs better than I expected, and makes home haircuts quick and stress-free.

So far, so good with this shaver! I’ll update this review if anything changes, but I wanted to share a comparison since I’m coming from the Manscaped Lawn Mower 4.0—which turned out to be a letdown after the battery gave out within a year.
If this shaver lasts even as long as that one did, I’ll be more than happy. For the price, I could buy three of these before matching what I paid for the Manscaped.

Pros:
Great cutting power with a strong, steady motor. Unlike the Manscaped, which seemed to fluctuate in speed, this one stays consistent.
Much cheaper, and arguably more effective at cutting.

Cons (mostly comparing features):
It nicks a bit more, but I think that’s just because it cuts better and I was used to the duller Manscaped performance.
I miss the LED light from the Manscaped—helped in low-light grooming.
No wireless charging (though with the base it's not much different), and I wish the charging base used USB-C like the Manscaped’s did.

Still, overall, I’m impressed with the performance and value so far. Definitely feels like a smarter buy.

It works very well, my only downside with this though it it cuts my skin in a certain spot, like badly, not enough to go to the hospital. Other than that, it works very well. Its easy to clean and better than anything id buy at TJ Max or any personal hygiene store. Id buy this if I was older or younger, as a guy, this is perfect for making things feel smooth and its really great for trimming my beard. I wish it had more attachments, but on top of all that, it works really well. In the shower or where ever you do your thing. I WILL SAY THIS, if you are trimming in the shower and have to clean it, IT WILL BE MORE DIFFICULT THEN CLEANING IT DRY. It holds a charge really well too, ive had this for just about a week and a half and its not a poor battery from china that explodes when its on the charger for too long. Its cheap but id personally wanna try higher end brands for the trimming that I do.

For the love of your hairy mansack use with caution!! After reading the description and reviews I thought I would take a chance on this cut free product. I couldn't fault the delivery as it arrived the same day. Bonus I thought a thought I lived to quickly regret! With eager anticipation I opened the package and set this gift from the hairless God's on charge. The charge time was fast and the product looked the part so with baited breath I took hold of this item and went 'downtown'.... The first few strokes were like a magician using a wand, pure magic! The trimmer effortless cut through its task in hand with precision and luring me into a sense of security that only can be achieved after several mishaps of various degrees. Oh little magic trimmer what a wonderful false sense of glee you were providing. With a new sense of bravery and belief like no other I went a little further south to smooth out the mansack like never before..

Holy Mother of Jesus hear my prayers!! Save me from this massacre that was unfolding before my eyes!! The trimmer took off its sheep's clothing and revealed the real evil within! With precision it ate up my mansack with ruthlessness never witness before. The cuts were quick and endless, the snagged skin becoming more and more frequent. The blood dripping now was making the task at hand more difficult but undeterred by the great reviews I kept going believing it was my fault just a bad technique. We will prevail! Alas prevail turned into survival and a soon to be found fetal position praying for my man parts to heal from their recent brush with a back street castration. I'm sure it's just operator error and maybe once healed I will accept the challenge again  to wield this clippers and be the smooth operator it promised. In the meantime I would recommend buying several large packets of bandaid to get you through the blood donation you're about to give. On a plus side it cleaned up pretty nice and easy so all in all I'll give it 5 stars along with the body parts it took all by itself!!

Okay, I want to address the other reviews on here after using this myself. For all the people claiming that the SkinSafe Foil Blade broke on them, either yall don’t take care of yours OR you’re using it to rough. Worked like a charm for me and cuts extra smooth. With that being said, I don’t use mine in the shower. Even if it says waterproof, I never fully submerse in water. As far as the original head that comes on it, it’s give that 5 stars as well because I use that before I use the foil blade and it damn near does the job itself. The battery life is awesome! Super long lasting. This thing live us to its name as far as the cut, “The Lawn Mower.” I grow long straight hair and it cuts it with no problem.

If you’re thinking about buying this product, DO IT! I almost didn’t get it due to the 1,2, and 3 star reviews but now I’m convinced they just don’t know how to cut or take care of their things because their pictures show hair all up in their clippers. YOU GOTTA CLEAN THIS THING IN ORDER FOR IT TO WORK LIKE GOLD EVERY TIME! Laziness never will get you good results.

I’d rate this thing a 100/10. Buy it.

Love it! 46 year old male, first time sack shaver. I decided if I'm going to do it, I'm going for something designed for it, rather than going right for a razor. It did NOT disappoint!  Premium feel, shaves like a dream. Lightweight. Battery lasts plenty long enough. I didn't realize I'd love the light so much, bit it makes it SO much easier to use. The hardest part, figuring out how to coordinate myself while pulling things tight! Nothing to do with the "Lawn Mower", just a first timer getting used to shaving down there! I may ask for some help from the Mrs. and make it a team sport, cause I felt like I needed 3 hands! Did knick a few times, hurt less then I thought - but it was a user error. I'm right handed, so shaving with my left was a big struggle. The foil shaver got very close, I'd say razor close. That was something I was worried about as I need to be shaved for an upcoming procedure. I used in the shower as well, worked like a charm! Also used the recommended "Crop Gel" by Manscaped, amazing combo! Moisturized, smells great, NOT like petroleum jelly like some people claim - I'd order both! This first timer is impressed - I HIGHLY RECOMMEND!

This men’s grooming razor has worked really well for me. It gives a close, smooth shave and glides easily without pulling or irritating my skin. I also like that it hasn’t nicked or cut me while using it, which has been a problem with other razors I’ve tried. The handle feels comfortable to hold and gives good control while shaving. Overall, it’s a solid razor that makes shaving quick and easy without any hassle.

I have had this for a little over a year now and hands down the best trimmer I have ever owned! No nicks, no cuts no issues. Barely makes a sound and has more than enough power on its base setting to get the job done! Battery is a beast and rarely needs to be charged as well. I leave it on my shelf in the shower so not need to baby it. Had a little issue recently (after a year), contacted customer service and after a couple emails they got me all fixed up. Not only is it a GREAT product, they 100% stand behind it! Don't waste your money on the cheap ones, this is the last one you will need to buy.

I purchased the MANSCAPED Lawn Mower 5.0 Ultra for grooming body hair and sensitive areas, and after using it regularly, it’s been a noticeable upgrade from basic trimmers. It’s easy to handle, feels well-built, and does a good job balancing performance with comfort.

What I Like:
• Dual-head design makes trimming and finishing easier
• SkinSafe blades help reduce irritation and nicks
• Waterproof design works well for wet or dry use
• Built-in LED light is helpful for visibility
• Battery life is solid and charges quickly with USB-C

What Could Be Better:
• Takes a little practice to get the closest trim without multiple passes

Yes it is a razor, yes it can cause some irritation on the boys. But overall it’s the smoothest and easiest trimmer for downstairs.

I used it on my chest and abdomen and with a couple of passes in each direction the body and balls razor worked extremely well. Granted that area was previously trimmed a few days ago but it definitely got all the hair the other razor missed or wouldn’t cut.

The size is decent. I feel that the design is well balanced and holding it in various positions is easy.

The blades are sharp and smooth. Again it is a razor so it can cut you. I did end up nicking my balls but the size of the cut paled in comparison to other razors.

Massive upgrade from the Wahl clippers I was using that was supposed to work for both body and facial hair. Anytime I used these Wahl clippers on my sensitive bits, I would cut myself.

This Philips Norelco Body Groomer set works extremely well and as advertised. No cuts, nicks, or scapes when I shaved downstairs. I was also very impressed by the guard that these came installed with. I was able to shave my man bits 10x faster than before, and was able to press down pretty hard without fear of breaking skin.

Best trimmer I have gotten for downstairs use, and I highly recommend

TLDR:
Blades are dull
Build quality is bottom of the barrel
Left me covered in burns and bumps and a sad trim
DO NOT BUY if you have any significant amount of hair

First of all a lot of the initial 5 star pictures you see are not of the same trimmer that you’re ordering here.

I tried a manscaped trimmer and was like damn this whole manscaping business isn’t all that bad. I can get a close trim without any pain or suffering and it’s relatively easy to do.

BUT I hated the idea of hopping on the marketing bandwagon of manscaped. I’m not that kind of guy. So I order this Phillips Norelco thinking it’ll be just as good, potentially better, without all the hype.

I was absolutely wrong.

The blades may as well be microscopic butter knives. The amount of effort to get through my relatively thin body hair was atrocious. I was left covered in bumps and irritation. I disinfected the razor and made sure to immediately cleanse myself and apply some moisturizer and that did nothing to save me. Now it’ll take a couple weeks for my skin to recover.

The build quality is low. Lightweight plastic body. Low quality metal parts. I wouldn’t trust it to survive a drop in my tub. It’s a miracle it’s considered waterproof.

I don’t hate Phillips norelco. I’ve had their products before. But my god is this thing terrible. Doesn’t live up to the name.

I hate to say it but I’m buying a manscaped trimmer. They feel infinitely smoother.

Works well at first with a lot of attachments, started losing effectiveness after shaving a bit more than half my body for the very first time. The machine still worked and was on, but it stopped actually shaving at least 80% of the hairs on my leg. I used an attachment and it worked on one leg, then for the other leg it just wasn’t shaving the hairs. I didn’t expect it to degrade so quickly, didn’t even get to finish shaving my body because it just wasn’t working. The foil shaver default attachment is good though.
Very nice works great and has plenty of trimming heads.
Works great as a beard / body trimmer and give a close shave too
This product is excellent and the price is very reasonable. Thank you to the manufacturer of this company.

I mistakenly followed the advice from a Reddit thread and purchased this disaster of a grooming device. The thing vibrates louder than something out of your sister’s toy box. My apartment complex can hear me three doors down trying to tame the complexities of its design.

Not worth $100. Not even close.

I have been using the 5000 series for many years and needed to replace one after I dropped it and the 5000 wasn't available so I bought this.  I use my razors every day.  This is less than a month old and ready for the garbage.  It shakes really bad.  Makes a ton of noise,  not very comfortable to use, and doesn't shave well until you can get it to stop shaking.  It actually stings on sensitive skin until you can get enough pressure to stop it from shaking.  All the power is going into shaking the plastic head instead of the blades.  If you hold the head with your finger tips it works fine but that is not easy to do.  The 5000's that I have been using for years use the same basic blade (maybe just a different color plastic) and work fantastic.  I have gotten several years of everyday use from them.  I went to the Philips Store on Amazon, found one, and ordered it.  I will order another as a spare.

Best groomer I have had. Many say they wont bit your bits. These actually wont and still get the job done. Worth the investment if you want to groom without razer bumps and nicks. No issues using it in the shower. Battery seems great so far. Worth the money because you will actually gladly use it. I also have the hair version. It has more guards and non rounded tip blades so it cuts through dencer hair better on your head and beard.

This is the first and only electric razor I will ever recommend. I’ve gone through every kind that exists and this is the only one that doesn’t cause irritation. The only down side is you pay for things you don’t need/want (eg extender), and there’s no way to clean hair out of the foil head so…..I guess ya just wait till it fails?

I’ve tried many body groomers and this one consistently gives me extensive razor burn no matter what I do or how lightly I press, much more than other body groomers. Otherwise would be a great device with the light, multiple attachments, and extender to use on your back.

Luckily, (for Olov), this Olov hair trimmer set doesn't actually call itself a "Groin and Body Hair trimmer on the box.  Seems like that would be a memorable but not very marketable description.  I called it a crotch shaver, an even less elegant name, when describing it to a friend.  It has a good heft, and looks like a quality product.  Its size and few attachments clearly lean toward this being a trimmer of various body "nooks and crannies".  The photo shows my assortment of trimmers, from a full fledged barber model, a beard and mustache trimmer, and the Olov.

I decided not to risk my "nooks and crannies" on the first trial of the Olov trimmer, giving it instead to my "Covid barber" (my wife) for my latest haircut.  It worked okay, but it grabbed the odd hair four or five times, about the same as the regular barber's trimmer.  I had hoped that since it was intended for use in "nooks and crannies," that it might do better than that.  The directions do mention combing the hair to be trimmed beforehand, a very good idea.

The Olov set comes with a charging stand,  ready for a spot on a bathroom counter.  I wish it had a couple more comb attachments, at least one that was shorter.  Some of the crannies might end up a little longer than I'd prefer.  All in all, a good looking useable tool.

Serviceable to knock down bulk, but does not get the hair as close to the skin as I would like. The shave leaves about a millimeter of wiry stubble and depending on the area, this can still be felt by my partner. A cheaper plastic philips personal groomer gets closer for me on the base of the shaft. Would likely not recommend this product if you're looking for a detailing groomer.
Just received this and literally shaved right away! Been growing out beard for 5 days to test it out. It does not grab and I was so relieved.

I've tested countless body trimmers, and this Purple Groin & Body Hair Trimmer has earned its five stars by simply doing what others promise but fail to deliver: a painless, nick-free trim in sensitive areas.

What I Love (The Real Highlights):
Ceramic Blades Are a Game Changer: This is the core strength. I used it on my most sensitive skin with no guard, and there was zero pulling, tugging, or painful nicks. It's incredibly gentle yet powerful.

LED Spotlight: Sounds silly, but the built-in light is brilliant. It illuminates hard-to-see areas, making the whole process faster and safer.

USB-C Charging: Finally, a groomer that uses a universal cable! The dock is convenient, and the battery lasts for multiple full-body sessions.

Fully Waterproof: I use it in the shower often. It cuts better on wet hair, and cleanup is as simple as running it under the tap.

Bottom Line
If you're upgrading from a standard trimmer that leaves you patchy and nicked, this is the one. It handles body hair everywhere—chest, legs, and especially the delicate areas—with total confidence and comfort. Highly recommend it for anyone serious about hygiene and avoiding painful mistakes.

This is a great trimmer for men! My husband said it is very gentle on the skin, avoids  irritation and works very well on all areas including the beard, chest, and legs. Overall its performance has been excellent, and it’s a great value for the price.

I mainly use it to trim my sideburns and remove nose hair. it cuts really well. It’s convenient to take on trips since it lasts a long time on a single charge. I like that it’s lightweight and has a cool light feature.

This OLOV trimmer is amazing! It’s powerful, precise and works great on any hair! Body, chest, legs, or even more sensitive areas. My husband has been using this trimmer and it works well for what it’s designed for. The ceramic blade feels gentle on the skin and seems to reduce the risk of nicks, which is especially important for sensitive areas. It’s lightweight, easy to handle, and the wireless charging dock is convenient.

Overall it’s a solid grooming tool that gets the job done and feels reasonably well made for the price.

As a woman, I can attest this is a great body hair trimmer set but think it would work great on men too.  For a rechargeable it has good power and seems to be holding a charge well, I've used it for a couple months now on the first charge but I'm not trimming a large area.  It comes with good attachments and I don't know if "no nick groin ball" is the technical term but it hasn't nicked anything on me so it's true.
